parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Jobs that have a Job name associated, e.g. equal to "name=name1" or contains "name=< like>name"

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alerts that have the specified description e.g. equal to "description=description1" or contains "description=< like>description"

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that has the following severity: NORMAL, INFORMATION, MINOR, WARNING, CRITICAL, FATAL

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that has the following state: NEW, ACKNOWLEDGED, CLEARED

parameters.name: created_date
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that are greater than(">1"), Less than("<1") or equal to the specified created_date "MMM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss.SSS"

parameters.name: created_date_milliseconds
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that contain greater than(">1"), Less than("<1") or equal to the specified date in milliseconds

parameters.name: acknowledged
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that are acknowledged or not  (true/false)

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that are associated with the specified array e.g. equal to "array=000213234443" or contains "description=<like>443"

parameters.name: object
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that are associated with the specified array object e.g. equal to "object=SRP_3" or contains "description=<like>_3"

parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Alert Ids that are associated with the specified array object type e.g. equal to "object_type=Symmetrix" or contains "description=<like>Symm"

put.description:
**Updates a password for a local Unisphere user account.** The password can be changed by any user given the supplied credentials are correct.

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning authorization rules management, authentication configuration and host-based access control.

RELATED ENDPOINTS

**Authentication Configuration**
  • [104/settings/access_control/authentication](https://developer.dell.com/apis/4458/versions/10.3.0/openapi.json/paths/~1103~1settings~1access_control~1authentication/put) - manages authentication configuration
**Role Based Access Management Control (RBAC) Configuration**
  • [104/settings/symmetrix/{symmetrixId}/access_control/authorization_rule](https://developer.dell.com/apis/4458/versions/10.3.0/openapi.json/paths/~1103~1settings~1symmetrix~1%7BsymmetrixId%7D~1access_control~1authorization_rule/post) - manages role based access control configuration
**Host Access Control Configuration**

get.description:
Lists host access ID mappings to a host name. The endpoint facilitates further authorization configuration to assign rights to individual management hosts that limit the management operations that can be performed from the host.

RELATED ENDPOINTS

**Host Access Control Configuration**
  • [104/settings/symmetrix/{symmetrixId}/access_control/host_access](https://developer.dell.com/apis/4458/versions/10.3.0/openapi.json/paths/~1103~1settings~1symmetrix~1%7BsymmetrixId%7D~1access_control~1host_access/get) - lists host access control configuration for the storage array

post.description:
Attach host ids to host names for a storage array. The endpoint facilitates applying further authorization configuration by assigning rights to individual management hosts, that will limit the management operations that can be performed from the host. Symmetrix Access Control identifies individual management hosts using unique access IDs, which are stored in a Lockbox. The Lockbox is associated with a particular host, which prevents copying the Lockbox from one host to another.

There are two different methods for generating the access IDs:
  • Alternate access ID - The host access ID can be generated at random or from a user-defined passphrase, then stored in a secure location on the local disk.
  • Hardware-based access ID (default) - The host access ID is derived from hardware characteristics of that host:
    • On x86_64 (64-bit Intel or AMD), and IA-64 platforms, a network interface MAC address is used.
    • On other platforms, characteristics of the host, such as a processor identifier, are used.
Alternate access IDs are available for all platforms. When alternate access IDs are enabled, Solutions Enabler can:
  • Randomly generate an access ID.
  • Generate an access ID based on a user-chosen passphrase, where the passphrase is either:
    • Entered on the command line in an option
    • Entered in a file, whose name is specified in the command line
Enable alternate access IDs with the `SYMAPI_ALTERNATE_ACCESS_ID` option in the `/config/options file`. Solutions Enabler securely stores the alternate access ID on the local disk in the Lockbox file. The symacl man page provides more information about the `symacl �unique` command.

RELATED ENDPOINTS

**Host Access Control Configuration**
  • [104/settings/symmetrix/{symmetrixId}/access_control/host_access](https://developer.dell.com/apis/4458/versions/10.3.0/openapi.json/paths/~1103~1settings~1symmetrix~1%7BsymmetrixId%7D~1access_control~1host_access/get) - lists host access control configuration for the storage array

get.description:
Lists alert policy configuration settings per storage array. **Alert Polices listed are ALL policies available in the Unisphere release version. Policies that are applicable to the storage array have ```applicable``` property set to ```true```.** Single alert policy that controls whether Unisphere alerts on event that occurred on the system. In order for a specific event(s) associated with a policy to be monitored on the system, the policy needs to be set to ```enabled```.

In order to be notified about the alerts being generated, notification settings need to be configured at the time of the event occurring on the system i.e. as the Unisphere alert is being generated. Alert notification settings include both: notification preferences per policy and the notification settings including notification target(s) configuration (smtp, snmp, syslog) including alert severity filtering per storage array.

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning notification configuration, viewing and managing the generated alerts.

put.description:
Updates alert policy configuration settings for a system. When a policy is ```enabled```, event(s) associated with the policy are being monitored by Unisphere instance.

Once an event has occurred on the system a corresponding alert is being genereated by Unisphere.

In order to be notified about the alerts being generated, notification settings need to be configured at the time of the event occurring on the system i.e. as the Unisphere alert is being generated. Alert notification settings include both: notification preferences per policy and the notification settings including notification target(s) configuration (smtp, snmp, syslog) including alert severity filtering per storage array.

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning notification configuration, viewing and managing the generated alerts.

put.description:
**Updates LDAP-SSL authentication configuration**

Only **_LDAP-SSL_** authority configuration is supported via REST API. Once configured and enabled, **_LDAP_**, or **_Lightweight Directory Access Protocol_** allows for centralized user management, making it easier to handle authentication across your PowerMax environment.When configuring LDAP Configuration and using a ssl certificate, this certificate must be given in base64 format with no newline or space characters.

On a Unix or Linux computer this can be accomplished by using the base64 command ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file -w 0`` or ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file | tr-d`` for versions of Linux which dont support ``base64 -w 0``. It is recommended that you write the output to a file e.g. ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file -w 0 > /path/to/certificate_file.b64``. On a Windows machine converting to base64 can be accomplished by using the following python script. ```python import os import base64 import sys if len(sys.argv) > 1: filename=sys.argv[1] if not os.path.isfile(filename): print("File %s doesnt exist") exit(1) else: print("No file given to encode") exit(1) def encode(file_name): with open(file_name, "rb") as file: file_content = base64.b64encode(file.read()) print(file_content) print(len(file_content)) with open(f"{file_name}.b64", 'w+') as b64_out: b64_out.writelines(file_content.decode("utf-8")) if __name__ == '__main__': encode(filename) ``` ### Linux Example When using bash scripting to call configure LDAP you can follow the example below. #### Example Json ldapBody.json ```json { "action": "Enable", "enable_ldap_authority": { "server": "fake.ldap.dell.com", "port": 4321, "bind_dn": "Cn=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=hop,DC=ad,DC=local", "bind_password": "fakePassword", "user_search_path": "DC=hop,DC=ad,DC=local", "user_object_class": "user", "user_id_attribute": "Samaccountname", "group_search_path": "DC=hop,DC=ad,DC=local", "group_name_attribute": "cn", "group_member_attribute": "member", "group_object_class": "group", "ssl_certificate": "$SSL_CERTIFICATE_B64", "limit_authentication_to_ldap_group_members": false, "ldap_group_names": "" } } ``` #### Example Bash Script ldapCurlConfigure.sh ```bash #!/bin/bash CERT=ldapServerCertificate.crt ENCODED_CERT=ldapCert.b64 echo "Encoding $CERT to base64 and saving to $ENCODED_CERT" base64 $CERT -w 0 > $ENCODED_CERT echo "Reading in $ENCODED_CERT and storing in variable "'$SSL_CERTIFICATE_B64'"" export SSL_CERTIFICATE_B64=$(<$ENCODED_CERT) echo "Substituting the "'$SSL_CERTIFICATE_B64'" variable in ldapBody.json with envsubst and saving the resulting json body in ldapBodyFilled.json" envsubst < ldapBody.json > ldapBodyFilled.json echo "LDAP GET Command" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X GET https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/access_control/authentication echo "" echo "LDAP PUT Command which uses ldapBodyFilled.json for the data being sent" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X PUT -H "Content-Type:application/json" -H "Accept: application/json" https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/access_control/authentication -d @ldapBodyFilled.json echo "" ```

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning local user management, RBAC configuration and host-based access control.

get.description:
**Lists authorization rules for the storage array.**

**_Role Based Access Control (RBAC)_** is a critical framework for managing user permissions and access to resources within an organization. The access is managed per storage array. Authorization rules can be defined for individual users or a user group, which is specified with `account_type` value.

In order to identify an individual user or group values such as: `name`, `authority` and `qualifier` are required. The `qualifier` specifies a **_domain_** or in case of a host-based access a **_host_** **_name_**.

Supported `authority` types for Unisphere REST API are: `localDirectory`, `host`, `ldapSSL`, `windowsAD` and `sso` (Single Sign-On).

In terms of user privileges, Unisphere supports the following user roles:
  • **Admin** - full access to all features and settings. Can manage user accounts and permissions.
  • **Storage Administrator** - limited access to user management. Can perform storage management tasks.
  • **Security Administrator** - limited access to user management. Can perform application security management tasks.
  • **Mainframe Administrator** - limited access to user management. Grants user rights to perform mainframe control and configuration operations on Splits and CU images. This role also grants the rights to perform z/OS Map and Unmap operations on CKD devices. This role also includes Monitor rights for non-mainframe operations.
  • **Monitor** - read-only access to monitor system status. Cannot make changes to configurations.
  • **Auditor** - grants the ability to view, but not modify, security settings for a storage system (including reading the audit log, symacl list, and symauth) in addition to all Monitor operations. It is the minimum role that is required to view the storage system audit log.
  • **Performance Monitor** - read-only access to monitor system performance. Cannot make changes to configurations.
  • **Local Replication** - performs local replication operations (SnapVX or legacy Snapshot, Clone, BCV). To create Secure SnapVX snapshots, a user must have Storage Admin rights at the storage system level. This role also includes Monitor rights.
  • **Remote Replication** - performs remote replication (SRDF) operations involving devices and pairs. Users can create, operate upon, or delete SRDF device pairs but cannot create, modify, or delete SRDF groups. This role also includes Monitor rights.
  • **Device Management** - grants user rights to perform control and configuration operations on devices. Storage Admin rights are required to create, expand, or delete devices. This role also includes Monitor rights.
  • **none** - revokes any privileges
Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning local user management, authentication configuration and host-based access control.

RELATED ENDPOINTS

**Local User Configuration**
  • [104/settings/access_control/local_user](https://developer.dell.com/apis/4458/versions/10.3.0/openapi.json/paths/~1103~1settings~1access_control~1local_user/put) - updates password for a local user
**Authentication Configuration**
  • [104/settings/access_control/authentication](https://developer.dell.com/apis/4458/versions/10.3.0/openapi.json/paths/~1103~1settings~1access_control~1authentication/put) - manages authentication configuration
**Host Access Control Configuration**
  • [104/settings/symmetrix/{symmetrixId}/access_control/host_access](https://developer.dell.com/apis/4458/versions/10.3.0/openapi.json/paths/~1103~1settings~1symmetrix~1%7BsymmetrixId%7D~1access_control~1host_access/get) - lists host access control configuration for the storage array

parameters.name: account_type
parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Authorization Rules based on the account type. Possible values are "user" and "group"

parameters.description: Optional value that filters returned list to display Authorization Rules based on the role e.g. "role=admin"

get.description:
Lists system threshold configuration settings per storage array. **System thresholds listed are ALL thresholds available in the Unisphere release version. Thresholds that are applicable to the storage array have ```applicable``` property set to ```true```.** Single system threshold controls whether Unisphere alerts on a component if monitored values are out of range. The threshold can be based on: - storage array - monitors a specific aspect of the storage array ex. Local Replication Utilization - storage resource pool (SRP) - monitors specific aspects of all SRPs ex. SRP Effective Used Capacity. SRP thresholds are managed separately for different emulations (FBA, CKD) - storage container - monitors utilization of all storage containers - storage group - monitors utilization of a storage group (not supported via REST API) Unisphere predefines default threshold values (typically 60-80-95 for SRP based thresholds and 60-80-100 for the remaining thresholds). Those values can be changed via REST API. To customize value for a specific instance of a managed component for example a specific storage container ex. SC_1, a custom threshold needs to be defined in Unisphere GUI.

In order to be notified about the alerts being generated, notification settings need to be configured at the time of the event occurring on the system i.e. as the Unisphere alert is being generated. Alert notification settings include both: notification preferences per policy and the notification settings including notification target(s) configuration (smtp, snmp, syslog) including alert severity filtering per storage array.

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning notification configuration, viewing and managing the generated alerts.

**Please note that in addition to this configuration, the storage array specific notification configuration may be required (email subscriptions, alert severity filtering). post.description:
Manages notification configuration for the Unisphere instance. Alert notification configuration may include SMTP server details, SNMP target(s) and syslog target. > The provided configuration will overwrite the existing one

When configuring SNMPv3 targets a security model option must be supplied, either "usm" or "tsm". For adding a standard SNMPv3 "usm" must be selected and the authorization parameters must also be supplied with the Username, Password and Passphrase of the SNMPv3 Target. For adding a SNMPv3 with TLS "tsm" must be selected and in the tls paramaeter the targets certificate must be given in a base64 format.

When configuring the Syslog target and using the protocol "stcp" a server certificate from the target must also be supplied in the "tls" parameter. This certificate must be given in base64 format.

On a Unix or Linux computer this can be accomplished by using the base64 command ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file - w 0`` or `` /path/to/certificate_file | tr-d`` for versions of linux which dont support ``base64 -w 0``. On a Windows machine converting to base64 can be accomplished by using the following python script. ```python import os import base64 import sys if len(sys.argv) > 1: filename=sys.argv[1] if not os.path.isfile(filename): print("File %s doesnt exist") exit(1) else: print("No file given to encode") exit(1) def encode(file_name): with open(file_name, "rb") as file: file_content = base64.b64encode(file.read()) print(file_content) print(len(file_content)) with open(f"{file_name}.b64", 'w+') as b64_out: b64_out.writelines(file_content.decode("utf-8")) if __name__ == '__main__': encode(filename) ``` ### Linux Example When using bash scripting to call configure SNMPv3 with TLS you can follow the example below. #### Example Json snmpBody.json ```json { "snmp": { "snmp_trap_targets": [ { "host": "10.40.116.136", "port": 163, "version": 3, "security_model": "tsm", "authorization": { "certificate_authority_pem":"$SNMP_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64" } } ] } } ``` #### Example Bash Script snmpV3TsmCurlConfigure.sh ```bash #!/bin/bash CERT=snmpServerCertificate.crt ENCODED_CERT=snmpCert.b64 echo "Encoding $CERT to base64 and saving to $ENCODED_CERT" base64 $CERT -w 0 > $ENCODED_CERT echo "Reading in $ENCODED_CERT and storing in variable "'$SNMP_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64'"" export SNMP_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64=$(<$ENCODED_CERT) echo "Substituting the "'$SNMP_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64'" variable in snmpBody.json with envsubst and saving the resulting json body in snmpBodyFilled.json" envsubst < snmpBody.json > snmpBodyFilled.json echo "SNMP GET Command" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X GET https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/alert/notification echo "" echo "SNMP POST Command which uses snmpBodyFilled.json for the data being sent" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X POST -H "Content-Type:application/json" -H "Accept: application/json" https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/alert/notification -d @snmpBodyFilled.json echo "" ``` **SMTP Configuration**
  • server: This can be an IP address or a 255 character limit hostname.
  • port: The port number in the range of 1 to 65536.
  • sender: The sender email address
  • host: This can be an IP address or a 255 character limit hostname.
  • port: The port number in the range of 1 to 65536.
  • protocol: The protocol can be TCP, UDP or STCP (Secure TCP)
  • text_format: The text_format can be RFC_3164, RFC_5424 or RFC_5425. Note that RFC_5425 is only applicable with protocol SCTP.
  • tls: This is the TLS certificate which must be in base64 format. TLS is only supported with STCP
    • ### Linux Example When using bash scripting to call configure Syslog with the protocol STCP you can follow the example below. #### Example Json syslogBody.json ```json { "syslog": { "syslog_target":{ "host":"10.60.141.115", "port":"6514", "protocol":"stcp", "text_format":"RFC_5425" , "tls":"$Syslog_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64" } } } ```

    #### Example Bash Script syslogV3TsmCurlConfigure.sh ```bash #!/bin/bash CERT=syslogServerCertificate.crt ENCODED_CERT=syslogCert.b64 echo "Encoding $CERT to base64 and saving to $ENCODED_CERT" base64 $CERT -w 0 > $ENCODED_CERT echo "Reading in $ENCODED_CERT and storing in variable "'$Syslog_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64'"" export Syslog_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64=$(<$ENCODED_CERT) echo "Substituting the "'$Syslog_TSM_CERTIFICATE_B64'" variable in syslogBody.json with envsubst and saving the resulting json body in syslogBodyFilled.json" envsubst < syslogBody.json > syslogBodyFilled.json echo "Syslog GET Command" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X GET https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/10/settings/alert/notification echo "" echo "Syslog POST Command which uses syslogBodyFilled.json for the data being sent" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X POST -H "Content-Type:application/json" -H "Accept: application/json" https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/alert/notification -d @syslogBodyFilled.json echo "" ``` **Please note that in addition to this configuration, the storage array specific notification configuration may be required (email subscriptions, alert severity filtering). Also in order to be notified on specific events, notification preferences need to be configured per alert policy or threshold associated with the event. See related endpoints for details.**

put.description:
**Blocks processing of calls to certain REST API endpoints.** Once the API has been disabled, any subsequent calls to the endpoints will not be processed. The API can be unblocked only from Unisphere GUI. The option to control the API is available in **_Settings_** dialog under **_Security Preferences_**.

The endpoints that are being disabled are responsible for authorization role based access control (RBAC) management. Please see **_related endpoints_** section for details.

RELATED ENDPOINTS

**Role Based Access Management Control (RBAC) Configuration**

get.description:
**Retrieves Secure Connect Gateway (SCG) configuration details.** SCG is a two-way remote connection between Dell EMC Customer Service and your Dell EMC products that enables remote monitoring, diagnosis, and repair. SCG assures availability and optimization of your Dell EMC infrastructure, and is a key component of Dell EMC's industry leading Customer Service.

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning SCG configuration

post.description:
**Configures Secure Connect Gateway (SCG).** SCG is a two-way remote connection between Dell EMC Customer Service and your Dell EMC products that enables remote monitoring, diagnosis, and repair. SCG assures availability and optimization of your Dell EMC infrastructure, and is a key component of Dell EMC's industry leading Customer Service.When configuring the SCG server and using a server certificate from the SCG server must also be supplied in the "server_cert" parameter. This certificate must be given in base64 format.

On a Unix or Linux computer this can be accomplished by using the base64 command ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file - w 0`` or `` /path/to/certificate_file | tr-d`` for versions of linux which dont support ``base64 -w 0``. On a Windows machine converting to base64 can be accomplished by using the following python script. ```python import os import base64 import sys if len(sys.argv) > 1: filename=sys.argv[1] if not os.path.isfile(filename): print("File %s doesnt exist") exit(1) else: print("No file given to encode") exit(1) def encode(file_name): with open(file_name, "rb") as file: file_content = base64.b64encode(file.read()) print(file_content) print(len(file_content)) with open(f"{file_name}.b64", 'w+') as b64_out: b64_out.writelines(file_content.decode("utf-8")) if __name__ == '__main__': encode(filename) ```

The connection is secure, high speed, and operates 24x7.The connection is also responsible for transporting data for the CloudIQ application which aggregates data from multiple Unisphere instances. ### Linux Example When using bash scripting to call configure SCG with a certificate you can follow the example below. #### Example Json scgBody.json ```json { "gateway_host": "12.12.12.12", "gateway_port": 9443, "access_key":"lorem.epsom@emconlinesupport.com", "pin":"loremEpsom", "server_cert":"$SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64" } ```

#### Example Bash Script scgCurlConfigure.sh ```bash #!/bin/bash CERT=scgServerCertificate.crt ENCODED_CERT=scgCert.b64 echo "Encoding $CERT to base64 and saving to $ENCODED_CERT" base64 $CERT -w 0 > $ENCODED_CERT echo "Reading in $ENCODED_CERT and storing in variable "'$SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64'"" export SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64=$(<$ENCODED_CERT) echo "Substituting the "'$SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64'" variable in scgBody.json with envsubst and saving the resulting json body in scgBodyFilled.json" envsubst < scgBody.json > scgBodyFilled.json echo "SCG GET Command" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X GET https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/registration/scg echo "" echo "SCG POST Command which uses scgBodyFilled.json for the data being sent" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X POST -H "Content-Type:application/json" -H "Accept: application/json" https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/registration/scg -d @scgBodyFilled.json echo "" ``` >Please note that the server certificate can be managed separately to the SCG configuration.Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning SCG configuration.

put.description:
Configures CA certificate validation for the Secure Connect Gateway (SCG). CA certificate can be managed separately to the SCG gateway setup, allowing to seamlessly update the certificate without affecting the SCG connection configuration. The "server_cert" must be provided as a base64 encoded string. On a Unix or Linux computer this can be accomplished by using the base64 command ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file -w 0`` or ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file | tr-d`` for versions of Linux which dont support ``base64 -w 0``. It is recommended that you write the output to a file e.g. ``base64 /path/to/certificate_file -w 0 > /path/to/certificate_file.b64``. On a Windows machine converting to base64 can be accomplished by using the following python script. ```python import os import base64 import sys if len(sys.argv) > 1: filename=sys.argv[1] if not os.path.isfile(filename): print("File %s doesnt exist") exit(1) else: print("No file given to encode") exit(1) def encode(file_name): with open(file_name, "rb") as file: file_content = base64.b64encode(file.read()) print(file_content) print(len(file_content)) with open(f"{file_name}.b64", 'w+') as b64_out: b64_out.writelines(file_content.decode("utf-8")) if __name__ == '__main__': encode(filename) ``` ### Linux Example When using bash scripting to call update SCG certificate you can follow the example below. #### Example Json scgBody.json ```json { "certificate_validation_enabled": true, "server_cert": "$SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64" } ``` #### Example Bash Script scgCurlUpdate.sh ```bash #!/bin/bash CERT=scgServerCertificate.crt ENCODED_CERT=scgCert.b64 echo "Encoding $CERT to base64 and saving to $ENCODED_CERT" base64 $CERT -w 0 > $ENCODED_CERT echo "Reading in $ENCODED_CERT and storing in variable "'$SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64'"" export SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64=$(<$ENCODED_CERT) echo "Substituting the "'$SCG_CERTIFICATE_B64'" variable in scgBody.json with envsubst and saving the resulting json body in scgBodyFilled.json" envsubst < scgBody.json > scgBodyFilled.json echo "SCG GET Command" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X GET https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/104/settings/registration/scg/server_cert echo "" echo "SCG POST Command which uses scgBodyFilled.json for the data being sent" curl -k -u fakeUser:fakePassword -a -basic -X PUT -H "Content-Type:application/json" -H "Accept: application/json" https://unispehreadresss.com:8443/univmax/restapi/10/settings/registration/scg/server_cert -d @scgBodyFilled.json echo "" ```

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning SCG configuration

get.description:
Lists break down of eligible storage groups according to its Service Level Objective (SLO) compliance policy settings. For the storage group to eligible for compliance alerting, it needs to fullfill certain condtions such as: - Be either a child or stand-alone. Parent storage groups are not supported - Be associated with a service level other than optimized - Contain volumes other than gatekeepers - Be in a masking view If a storage group is listed under `custom_Policies` it means that it has its own compliance policy settings that can be configured via Unisphere GUI. Once a storage group has been allocated to a group policy for a system, it's settings will be configurable via REST API.

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning notification configuration, viewing and managing the generated alerts.

put.summary: Update Storage Group Compliance Policy Allocation
put.description:
Controls allocation of eligible storage groups to a group policy per storage array which is configurable via REST API. Removing a storage group from the policy management means that Unisphere will not alert on Service Level Objective (SLO) compliance changes on the storage group. If a storage group is listed under `custom_Policies` it means that it has its own compliance policy settings that can be configured via Unisphere GUI. Once a storage group has been allocated to a group policy for a system, it's settings will be configurable via REST API under a group policy.

Please see the **_related endpoints_** section for endpoints concerning notification configuration, viewing and managing the generated alerts.

get.description: This call queries the system-component utilization values for the past two weeks. The utilization values shown are normalized to a best practice limit for each component type.  By default, only most limiting (highest utilization) value is shown for a given timestamp.

parameters.name: bucket
parameters.description: The bucket Id. Display all component utilization values for a given bucketId. A bucket is a four hour time window. A week has 42 buckets (0-indexed). Bucket 0 is Sunday 12am-4am, 1 is Sunday 4am-8am, etc

parameters.name: backEndOnly
parameters.description: Only show utilizations for back end components. Only back end components impact headroom calculations

health_status.title: health_status
health_status.description:
Health Value:
                        - 5: UNKNOWN - The health status cannot be determined (Object might be offline)
                        - 4: OK - The object works properly.
                        - 3: OK_BUT -  The object works but there is at least one issue that doesn't impact current functioning. For instance if the NAS server DNS client is configured with several servers but one doesn't work. The DNS client works but without degradation of service because only one server is used at a time. However this need to be fixed
                        - 2: DEGRADED - The object works but there is at least one issue that degrades its functioning. For instance if the NAS server Virus Checker is configured with several servers but one doesn't work, the Virus Checker works but there are less servers available to run parallel check.
                        - 1: CRITICAL - The object doesn't work and must be fixed. For instance, all configured DNS servers of the NAS Server DNS client don't work. In that case, the NAS server DNS client doesn't work.
                        - 0: NON_RECOVERABLE - The object doesn't work and cannot be fixed. This may be the case in case of unrecoverable file system corruption.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **NON_RECOVERABLE**
* **CRITICAL**
* **DEGRADED**
* **OK_BUT**
* **OK**
* **UNKNOWN**

username.title: username
username.description: Name of a Windows user allowing Events Publishing to connect to CEPA servers. To ensure that a secure connection (via Microsoft RPC protocol) is used disable HTTP by setting httpPort to 0

heartbeat.title: heartbeat
heartbeat.description: Time interval to scan each CEPA server (in seconds) for online/offline status

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds while attempting to send event to a CEPA server to determine that is offline

http_port.title: http_port
http_port.description:
TCP port number used but the service to connect to the CEPA server(s) with HTTP. Default port number is 12228.
                        Set this httpPort value to 0 to disable HTTP.
                        When enabled, connection via HTTP is attempted first.
                        If HTTP connection is disabled, or the connection fails, then connection through MSRPC is attempted if all CEPP server(s) are defined by FQDN.
                        The SMB account of the NAS server in the AD Domain is used to make the connection via MSRPC. Note that HTTP connections should only be used on secure networks, as it is neither SSL nor authenticated.

deny_access_when_all_servers_offline.title: deny_access_when_all_servers_offline
deny_access_when_all_servers_offline.description:
Behaviour when the File Event Service server did not answer. Values are:
                        false - indicates that nothing changes with I/O in case of File Event Service server is offline.
                        true - indicates that all I/O is denied in case of File Event Service server is offline.

post_event_policy.title: post_event_policy
post_event_policy.description:
Post-event notification policies for the Event Service. Values are:
                        - 0: Ignore - Continue and tolerate lost events.
                        - 1: Accumulate - Continue and use a persistence file as a circular event buffer for lost events.
                        - 2: Guarantee - Continue and use a persistence file as a circular event buffer for lost events until the buffer is filled and then deny access to files systems where Events Publishing is Enabled.
                        - 3: Deny - Deny access to files systems where Events Publishing is enabled.

id.description: Unique identifier of the Event publisher

enabled.title: enabled
enabled.description: enable the event publisher default: false

username.title: username
username.description: Name of a Windows user allowing Events Publishing to connect to CEPA servers. To ensure that a secure connection (via Microsoft RPC protocol) is used disable HTTP by setting httpPort to 0

heartbeat.title: heartbeat
heartbeat.description: Time interval to scan each CEPA server (in seconds) for online/offline status

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds while attempting to send event to a CEPA server to determine that is offline

http_port.title: http_port
http_port.description:
TCP port number used but the service to connect to the CEPA server(s) with HTTP. Default port number is 12228.
                        Set this httpPort value to 0 to disable HTTP.
                        When enabled, connection via HTTP is attempted first.
                        If HTTP connection is disabled, or the connection fails, then connection through MSRPC is attempted if all CEPP server(s) are defined by FQDN.
                        The SMB account of the NAS server in the AD Domain is used to make the connection via MSRPC. Note that HTTP connections should only be used on secure networks, as it is neither SSL nor authenticated.

deny_access_when_all_servers_offline.title: deny_access_when_all_servers_offline
deny_access_when_all_servers_offline.description:
Behaviour when the File Event Service server did not answer. Values are:
                        false - indicates that nothing changes with I/O in case of File Event Service server is offline.
                        true - indicates that all I/O is denied in case of File Event Service server is offline.

post_event_policy.title: post_event_policy
post_event_policy.description:
Post-event notification policies for the Event Service. Values are:
                        - 0: Ignore - Continue and tolerate lost events.
                        - 1: Accumulate - Continue and use a persistence file as a circular event buffer for lost events.
                        - 2: Guarantee - Continue and use a persistence file as a circular event buffer for lost events until the buffer is filled and then deny access to files systems where Events Publishing is Enabled.
                        - 3: Deny - Deny access to files systems where Events Publishing is enabled.

entries.title: entries
entries.description: List of the event publisher identifier

id.title: id
id.description: Unique Idenfier of the Event Publisher

eventPublisherIdentifier.description: Identifier of the Event Publisher Identifier

nas_server.description: Unique Identifier of NASServer

name.title: name
name.description: Name of the eventspool

events_publisher_servers.title: events_publisher_servers
events_publisher_servers.description:
Network identifier of the File Event Service servers. (fully qualified domain name or ip addresses).
                        You can set at max 5 File Event Service Servers per Events Pool.

items.title: events_publisher_servers
items.description:
Network identifier of the File Event Service servers. (fully qualified domain name or ip addresses).
                        You can set at max 5 File Event Service Servers per Events Pool.

override.title: override
override.description: In the context of a replication, tell if the eventsPublisherServers list has been overridden when this object is in destination mode

entries.title: entries
entries.description: List of the Events Pool Identifier

eventsPoolList.description: List of the Events Pool Identifier

name.title: name
name.description: Name of the eventspool

nas_server.title: nas_server
nas_server.description: nas_server

ftp_enabled.title: ftp_enabled
ftp_enabled.description:
Indicates whether the FTP server is enabled on the NAS server specified in the nasServer attribute. Values are:
                        - true - FTP server is enabled on the specified NAS server.
                        - false - FTP server is disabled on the specified NAS server.

sftp_enabled.title: sftp_enabled
sftp_enabled.description:
Indicates whether the SFTP server is enabled on the NAS server specified in the nasServer attribute. Values are:
                        - true - SFTP server is enabled on the specified NAS server.
                        - false - SFTP server is disabled on the specified NAS server.

smb_authentication_enabled.title: smb_authentication_enabled
smb_authentication_enabled.description:
Indicates whether FTP and SFTP clients can be authenticated using a SMB user name.
                        These user names are defined in a Windows domain controller, and their formats are "user(at)domain" or "domain(slash)user".
                        Values are:
                        - true - SMB user names are accepted for authentication.
                        - false - SMB user names are not accepted for authentication.

unix_authentication.title: unix_authentication
unix_authentication.description:
Indicates whether FTP and SFTP clients can be authenticated using a Unix user name.
                        Unix user names are defined in LDAP or NIS servers or in local passwd file.
                        Values are:
                        - true - Unix user names are accepted for authentication.
                        - false - Unix user names are not accepted for authentication.

anonymous_authentication.title: anonymous_authentication
anonymous_authentication.description:
Indicates whether FTP clients can be authenticated anonymously.
                        Values are:
                        - true - Anonymous user name is accepted.
                        - false - Anonymous user name is not accepted.

homedir_limit.title: homedir_limit
homedir_limit.description:
Indicates whether an FTP or SFTP user access is limited to his home directory. Values are:
                        - true - An FTP or SFTP user can access his or her own home directory only.
                        - false - FTP and SFTP users can access any NAS server directory, according to NAS server permissions.

default_homedir.title: default_homedir
default_homedir.description:
(Applies when the value of HomedirLimit is false.)
                        Default directory of FTP and SFTP clients who have a home directory that is not defined or accessible.
                        This parameter is an absolute path relative to the root of the NAS server.

welcome_msg.title: welcome_msg
welcome_msg.description: Welcome message displayed on the console of FTP and SFTP clients before their authentication.

message_of_the_day.title: message_of_the_day
message_of_the_day.description: Message of the day displayed on the console of FTP clients after their authentication.

audit_enabled.title: audit_enabled
audit_enabled.description:
Indicates whether the activity of FTP and SFTP clients is tracked in audit files. Values are:
                        - true - FTP/SFTP activity is tracked.
                        - false - FTP/SFTP activity is not tracked.

audit_dir.title: audit_dir
audit_dir.description:
(Applies when the value of AuditEnabled is true.)
                        Directory of FTP/SFTP audit files.
                        This parameter is an absolute path relative to the root of the NAS server.
                        This directory cannot be located on the root file system.

audit_max_size.title: audit_max_size
audit_max_size.description:
(Applies when the value of AuditEnabled is true.)
                        Maximum size of FTP/SFTP audit files, in bytes.
                        The maximum value for this setting is 1GB if the audit directory belongs to a user file system of the NAS server.
                        If it belongs to the config file system, the maximum value is 1MB.
                        The minimum value is 8kB on any file system.
                        When the audit file size reaches this value, the file is closed and renamed with an index. A new audit file is then created (files rotation).
                        Up to 4 renamed audit files are kept. the oldest files are deleted.

hosts_list.title: hosts_list
hosts_list.description:
Allowed or denied hosts, depending on the value of the AllowHosts attribute.
                        A host is defined using its IP address.
                        Subnets using CIDR notation are also supported.
                        - If allowed hosts exist, only those hosts and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied hosts exist, they always have access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the host IP.

items.title: hosts_list
items.description:
Allowed or denied hosts, depending on the value of the AllowHosts attribute.
                        A host is defined using its IP address.
                        Subnets using CIDR notation are also supported.
                        - If allowed hosts exist, only those hosts and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied hosts exist, they always have access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the host IP.

users_list.title: users_list
users_list.description:
Allowed or denied users, depending on the value of the AllowUsers attribute.
                        - If allowed users exist, only those users and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied users exist, they have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user name.

items.title: users_list
items.description:
Allowed or denied users, depending on the value of the AllowUsers attribute.
                        - If allowed users exist, only those users and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied users exist, they have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user name.

groups_list.title: groups_list
groups_list.description:
Allowed or denied user groups, depending on the value of the AllowGroups attribute.
                        - If allowed groups exist, only users who are members of these groups and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied groups exist, all users who are members of those groups have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user group.

items.title: groups_list
items.description:
Allowed or denied user groups, depending on the value of the AllowGroups attribute.
                        - If allowed groups exist, only users who are members of these groups and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied groups exist, all users who are members of those groups have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user group.

allow_hosts.title: allow_hosts
allow_hosts.description:
Indicates whether the hostsList attribute contains allowed or denied hosts. Values are:
                        true - hostsList contains allowed hosts.
                        false - hostsList contains denied hosts.
                        Note the hostsList must be empty to be able to change the value of this property.

allow_users.title: allow_users
allow_users.description:
Indicates whether the usersList attribute contains allowed or denied users. Values are:
                        - true - usersList contains allowed users.
                        - false - usersList contains denied users.
                        Note the usersList must be empty to be able to change the value of this property.

allow_groups.title: allow_groups
allow_groups.description:
Indicates whether the groupsList attribute contains allowed or denied user groups. Values are:
                        - true - groupsList contains allowed user groups.
                        - false - groupsList contains denied user groups.
                        Note the groupsList must be empty to be able to change the value of this property.

                        Directory of FTP/SFTP audit files.
                        This parameter is an absolute path relative to the root of the NAS server.
                        This directory cannot be located on the root file system.

audit_max_size.title: audit_max_size
audit_max_size.description:
(Applies when the value of AuditEnabled is true.)
                        Maximum size of FTP/SFTP audit files, in bytes.
                        The maximum value for this setting is 1GB if the audit directory belongs to a user file system of the NAS server.
                        If it belongs to the config file system, the maximum value is 1MB.
                        The minimum value is 8kB on any file system.
                        When the audit file size reaches this value, the file is closed and renamed with an index. A new audit file is then created (files rotation).
                        Up to 4 renamed audit files are kept. the oldest files are deleted.

hosts_list.title: hosts_list
hosts_list.description:
Allowed or denied hosts, depending on the value of the AllowHosts attribute.
                        A host is defined using its IP address.
                        Subnets using CIDR notation are also supported.
                        - If allowed hosts exist, only those hosts and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied hosts exist, they always have access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the host IP.

items.title: hosts_list
items.description:
Allowed or denied hosts, depending on the value of the AllowHosts attribute.
                        A host is defined using its IP address.
                        Subnets using CIDR notation are also supported.
                        - If allowed hosts exist, only those hosts and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied hosts exist, they always have access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the host IP.

users_list.title: users_list
users_list.description:
Allowed or denied users, depending on the value of the AllowUsers attribute.
                        - If allowed users exist, only those users and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied users exist, they have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user name.

items.title: users_list
items.description:
Allowed or denied users, depending on the value of the AllowUsers attribute.
                        - If allowed users exist, only those users and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied users exist, they have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user name.

groups_list.title: groups_list
groups_list.description:
Allowed or denied user groups, depending on the value of the AllowGroups attribute.
                        - If allowed groups exist, only users who are members of these groups and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied groups exist, all users who are members of those groups have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user group.

items.title: groups_list
items.description:
Allowed or denied user groups, depending on the value of the AllowGroups attribute.
                        - If allowed groups exist, only users who are members of these groups and no others can connect to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If denied groups exist, all users who are members of those groups have always access denied to the NAS server through FTP or SFTP.
                        - If the list is empty, there is no restriction to the NAS server access through FTP or SFTP based on the user group.

allow_hosts.title: allow_hosts
allow_hosts.description:
Indicates whether the hostsList attribute contains allowed or denied hosts. Values are:
                        true - hostsList contains allowed hosts.
                        false - hostsList contains denied hosts.
                        Note the hostsList must be empty to be able to change the value of this property.

allow_users.title: allow_users
allow_users.description:
Indicates whether the usersList attribute contains allowed or denied users. Values are:
                        - true - usersList contains allowed users.
                        - false - usersList contains denied users.
                        Note the usersList must be empty to be able to change the value of this property.

allow_groups.title: allow_groups
allow_groups.description:
Indicates whether the groupsList attribute contains allowed or denied user groups. Values are:
                        - true - groupsList contains allowed user groups.
                        - false - groupsList contains denied user groups.
                        Note the groupsList must be empty to be able to change the value of this property.

role.title: role
role.description:
Production network interfaces are used for all file protocols and services of NAS server. Backup network interfaces are used only for NDMP/NFS backup.
                        System network interfaces are reserved for system traffic such as for NAS server migration, they can't be used for the production traffic.
                        - 0 - Production
                        - 1 - Backup
                        - 2 - System
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Production**
* **Backup**
* **System**

                        - false - stop tracking usages for all users on a file system , and user quota limits will not be enforced.

mount_State

access_policy.title: access_policy
access_policy.description:
File system or protocol snap security access policies.
                        - 0: Native Security.
                        - 1: UNIX Security.
                        - 2: Windows Security.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Native**
* **UNIX**
* **Windows**

locking_policy.title: locking_policy
locking_policy.description:
File system or protocol snap locking policies. These policy choices control whether the NFSv4 range locks must be honored. Because NFSv3 is advisory by design, this policy allows specifying whether the NFSv4 locking feature behaves like NFSv3 (advisory mode) in order to be backward compatible with applications expecting an advisory locking scheme.
                        - 0: Advisory - No lock checking for NFS and honor SMB lock range only for SMB.
                        - 1: Mandatory - Honor SMB and NFS lock range.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Advisory**
* **Mandatory**

folder_rename_policy.title: folder_rename_policy
folder_rename_policy.description:
File system or protocol snap folder rename policies. These policy choices control whether directory can be renamed from NFS or SMB clients if at least one file is opened in the directory or in one of its child directories.
                        - 0: SMB_Rename_Forbidden - A directory rename from the SMB protocol will be denied if at least one file is opened in the directory or in one of its child directories.
                        - 1: All_Rename_Forbidden - Any directory rename request will be denied regardless of the protocol used, if at least one file is opened in the directory or in one of its child directories.
                        - 2: All_Rename_Allowed - All protocols are allowed to rename directories without any restrictions.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **SMB_Rename_Forbidden**
* **All_Rename_Forbidden**
* **All_Rename_Allowed**

host_ioblock_size.title: host_ioblock_size
host_ioblock_size.description: File system data block size (in KiB).

nas_server.title: nas_server
nas_server.description: Id of the NAS Server on which the file system is mounted

flr_mode.title: flr_mode
flr_mode.description:
The FLR type of the file system. This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **None**
* **Enterprise**
* **Compliance**

flr_min_ret.title: flr_min_ret
flr_min_ret.description:
The shortest retention period for which files on an FLR-enabled file system can be locked and protected from deletion. This value must be less than or equal to the maximum retention period. Any attempt to lock a file for less than the minimum retention period results in the file being locked until the current system time plus the minimum retention period is reached. The default value for the minimum retention period is 1 day.
                        Format [Y|M|D] (example 5Y for 5 years), specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or the keyword infinite. Setting infinite means that the files can never be deleted.  This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_def_ret.title: flr_def_ret
flr_def_ret.description:
The default retention period that is used in an FLR-enabled file system when a file is locked and a retention period is not specified. This value must be greater than or equal to the minimum retention period, and less than or equal to the maximum retention period.
                        Format [Y|M|D] (example 5Y for 5 years).  Specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or infinite. The default value for the default retention period is infinite, which means that the files can never be deleted.  This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_max_ret.title: flr_max_ret
flr_max_ret.description: The longest retention period for which files on an FLR-enabled file system can be locked and protected from deletion. Any attempt to lock a file for more than this maximum retention period results in the file being locked until the current system time plus the maximum retention period is reached. Specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or infinite. The default value for the maximum retention period is infinite, which means that the files can never be deleted. This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_auto_lock.title: flr_auto_lock
flr_auto_lock.description:
Indicates whether to automatically lock files in an FLR-enabled file system.  When true files are locked automatically after modification based on the flrPolicyInterval interval.  When enabled, auto-locked files are set with the default retention period value. Values:
                        - true - automatic lock is enabled on the file system.
                        - false - automatic lock is disabled on the file system.
                        This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_auto_delete.title: flr_auto_delete
flr_auto_delete.description:
Indicates whether locked files will be automatically delete from an FLR-enabled file system once their retention periods have expired. Values:
                        - true - automatic delete is enabled on the file system
                        - false - automatic delete is disabled on the file system.
                        This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_policy_interval.title: flr_policy_interval
flr_policy_interval.description: Indicates how long to wait (in seconds) after files are modified before the files are automatically locked. The default value is 3600 seconds (1 hour). This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_enabled.title: flr_enabled
flr_enabled.description: Indicates whether File Level Retention feature is enabled on the Filesystem.

flr_clock_time.title: flr_clock_time
flr_clock_time.description: Per file system clock used to track the retention date. It is initialized when an FLR-enabled file system is first mounted on a NAS Server. It does not advance when a file system is not mounted.  Format is RFC3339 and precision is in seconds (ie: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.000Z). This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_max_retention_date.title: flr_max_retention_date
flr_max_retention_date.description: Maximum date and time that has been set on any locked file in an FLR-enabled file system, which means that the file system itself will be protected until this date and time.  This value is compared to the FLR Clock Time to determine whether the date and time have passed.  Value is the string infinite if the maximum locked date is infinite, or a specific time in the RFC3339 format with precision in seconds (ie: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.000Z). This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.

flr_has_protected_files.title: flr_has_protected_files
flr_has_protected_files.description: Indicates whether FLR file system has protected files.

snap_policy_list.title: snap_policy_list
snap_policy_list.description: List of the IDs of all snap policies that this file system is attached to

items.title: snap_policy_list
items.description: List of the IDs of all snap policies that this file system is attached to

event_notifications.description:

                        - 1 : smb only notifications
                        - 2 : nfs only notifications
                        - 3 : smb and nfs notifications
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **off**
* **SMB**
* **NFS**
* **SMB_NFS**

info_threshold.title: info_threshold
info_threshold.description: The info threshold will trigger info event if the file system used space exceeds this threshold. Default value is 0 (disabled).

high_threshold.title: high_threshold
high_threshold.description: The high threshold will trigger info event if the file system used space exceeds this threshold. Default value is 75%. (set to 0 to disable)

warning_threshold.title: warning_threshold
warning_threshold.description: The warning threshold will trigger alert event if the file system used space exceeds this threshold. Default value is 95%. (set to 0 to disable)

data_reduction.title: data_reduction
data_reduction.description:
Platform attribute
                        - true if data reduction enabled
                        - false if data reduction disabled

quota_enabled.description:
Whether quota has to be enabled.
                        Quotas are not supported for read-only filesystems.
                        Values are:
                        - true - start tracking usages for all users on a file system or a quota tree, and user quota limits would be enforced.
                        - false - stop tracking usages for all users on a file system , and user quota limits will not be enforced.

grace_period.title: grace_period
grace_period.description:
Grace period of soft limits (seconds):
                        -1: default: infinite grace (Windows policy)
                        0: use system default of 1 week.
                        positive: grace period after which soft limit is treated as hard (seconds).

default_hard_limit.title: default_hard_limit
default_hard_limit.description:
Default hard limit of user quotas and tree quotas (bytes). Value is always rounded up to match the filesystem's physical block size.
                        (0 means 'No limitation'. This value can be used to compute amount of space consumed without limiting the space).

default_soft_limit.title: default_soft_limit
default_soft_limit.description:
Default soft limit of user quotas and tree quotas (bytes). Value is always rounded up to match the filesystem's physical block size.
                        (0 means 'No limitation')

host_iosize.title: host_iosize
host_iosize.description:
Typical size of writes from the server or other computer using the file system or LUN to the storage system. This setting is used to match the storage block size to the I/O of the primary application using the storage, which can optimize performance. Choose one of the suggested applications if you are using one of them, or check the setup guide or performance tuning information for the primary application, or stay with default 8K size for general use.  For create operation applies only to vmware datastore fstype filesystems:
                        - 8192: General_8K - Host I/O size is 8K for general purpose
                        - 16384: General_16K - Host I/O size is 16K for general purpose
                        - 32768: General_32K - Host I/O size is 32K for general purpose
                        - 65536: General_64K - Host I/O size is 64K for general purpose

flr_mode.title: flr_mode
flr_mode.description:
The FLR type of the file system. This setting is significant only if flrEnabled is true.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **None**
* **Enterprise**
* **Compliance**

flr_min_ret.title: flr_min_ret
flr_min_ret.description:
The shortest retention period for which files on an FLR-enabled file system can be locked and protected from deletion. This value must be less than or equal to the maximum retention period. Any attempt to lock a file for less than the minimum retention period results in the file being locked until the current system time plus the minimum retention period is reached.
                        Format [Y|M|D] (example 5Y for 5 years), specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or the keyword infinite. Setting infinite means that the files can never be deleted.  This attribute should be set only for FLR enabled filesystems.

flr_def_ret.title: flr_def_ret
flr_def_ret.description:
The default retention period that is used in an FLR-enabled file system when a file is locked and a retention period is not specified. This value must be greater than or equal to the minimum retention period, and less than or equal to the maximum retention period.
                        Format [Y|M|D] (example 5Y for 5 years).  Specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or infinite. The default value for the default retention period is infinite for Enterprise FLR mode, and 1 year for Compliance FLR mode.  This attribute should be set only for FLR enabled filesystems.

flr_max_ret.title: flr_max_ret
flr_max_ret.description: The longest retention period for which files on an FLR-enabled file system can be locked and protected from deletion. Any attempt to lock a file for more than this maximum retention period results in the file being locked until the current system time plus the maximum retention period is reached. Specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or infinite. setting infinite means that the files can never be deleted.  This attribute should be set only for FLR enabled filesystems.

flr_enabled.description: Indicates whether File Level Retention feature is to enabled on the Filesystem

locking_policy.title: locking_policy
locking_policy.description:
File system or protocol snap locking policies. These policy choices control whether the NFSv4 range locks must be honored. Because NFSv3 is advisory by design, this policy allows specifying whether the NFSv4 locking feature behaves like NFSv3 (advisory mode) in order to be backward compatible with applications expecting an advisory locking scheme.
                        - 0: Advisory - No lock checking for NFS and honor SMB lock range only for SMB.
                        - 1: Mandatory - Honor SMB and NFS lock range.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Advisory**
* **Mandatory**

folder_rename_policy.title: folder_rename_policy
folder_rename_policy.description:
File system or protocol snap folder rename policies. These policy choices control whether directory can be renamed from NFS or SMB clients if at least one file is opened in the directory or in one of its child directories.
                        - 0: SMB_Rename_Forbidden - A directory rename from the SMB protocol will be denied if at least one file is opened in the directory or in one of its child directories.
                        - 1: All_Rename_Forbidden - Any directory rename request will be denied regardless of the protocol used, if at least one file is opened in the directory or in one of its child directories.
                        - 2: All_Rename_Allowed - All protocols are allowed to rename directories without any restrictions.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **SMB_Rename_Forbidden**
* **All_Rename_Forbidden**
* **All_Rename_Allowed**

flr_min_ret.title: flr_min_ret
flr_min_ret.description:
The shortest retention period for which files on an FLR-enabled file system can be locked and protected from deletion. This value must be less than or equal to the maximum retention period. Any attempt to lock a file for less than the minimum retention period results in the file being locked until the current system time plus the minimum retention period is reached.
                        Format [Y|M|D] (example 5Y for 5 years), specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or the keyword infinite. Setting infinite means that the files can never be deleted.  This attribute should be set only for FLR enabled filesystems.

flr_def_ret.title: flr_def_ret
flr_def_ret.description:
The default retention period that is used in an FLR-enabled file system when a file is locked and a retention period is not specified. This value must be greater than or equal to the minimum retention period, and less than or equal to the maximum retention period.
                        Format [Y|M|D] (example 5Y for 5 years).  Specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or infinite. The default value for the default retention period is infinite for Enterprise FLR mode, and 1 year for Compliance FLR mode.  This attribute should be set only for FLR enabled filesystems.

flr_max_ret.title: flr_max_ret
flr_max_ret.description: The longest retention period for which files on an FLR-enabled file system can be locked and protected from deletion. Any attempt to lock a file for more than this maximum retention period results in the file being locked until the current system time plus the maximum retention period is reached. Specify Y for years, M for months, D for days, or infinite. setting infinite means that the files can never be deleted.  This attribute should be set only for FLR enabled filesystems.

flr_auto_lock.title: flr_auto_lock
flr_auto_lock.description:
Indicates whether to automatically lock files in an FLR-enabled file system.  When true files are locked automatically after modification based on the flrPolicyInterval interval.  When enabled, auto-locked files are set with the default retention period value. Values:
                        - true - automatic lock is enabled on the file system.
                        - false - automatic lock is disabled on the file system.
                        This setting can only be applied to mounted FLR enabled file systems.

flr_auto_delete.title: flr_auto_delete
flr_auto_delete.description:
Indicates whether locked files will be automatically delete from an FLR-enabled file system once their retention periods have expired. Values:
                        - true - automatic delete is enabled on the file system.
                        - false - automatic delete is disabled on the file system.
                        This setting can only be applied to mounted FLR enabled file systems.

flr_policy_interval.title: flr_policy_interval
flr_policy_interval.description: Indicates how long to wait (in seconds) after files are modified before the files are automatically locked. This setting can only be applied to mounted FLR enabled file systems.

info_threshold.title: info_threshold
info_threshold.description: The info threshold will trigger info event if the file system used space exceeds this threshold. Default value is 0 (disabled).

high_threshold.title: high_threshold
high_threshold.description: The high threshold will trigger info event if the file system used space exceeds this threshold. Default value is 75%. (set to 0 to disable)

warning_threshold.title: warning_threshold
warning_threshold.description: The warning threshold will trigger alert event if the file system used space exceeds this threshold. Default value is 95%. (set to 0 to disable)

computer_name.title: computer_name
computer_name.description:
Dns name of the associated Computer Account when the SMB server is joined to an Active Directory domain.
                        This name's minimum lenght is 2 characters, it is limited to 63 bytes and must not contain the following characters:
                        - comma (,)
                        - tilde (~)
                        - colon (:)
                        - exclamation point (!)
                        - at sign (@)
                        - number sign (#)
                        - dollar sign ($)
                        - percent (%)
                        - caret (^)
                        - ampersand ()
                        - apostrophe (')
                        - period (.) - note that if you enter string with period only the first word will be kept
                        - parentheses (())
                        - braces ({})
                        - underscore (_)
                        - white space (blank)
                        as definied by the Microsoft naming convention (see https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/909264/)

domain.title: domain
domain.description:
Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) where SMB server is registered in Active Directory.
                        (see https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/909264/)

organizational_unit.title: organizational_unit
organizational_unit.description: Organizational unit of the SMB server in Active Directory, if applicable.

netbios_name.title: netbios_name
netbios_name.description:
NetBios name is the network name of the standalone SMB server.
                        SMB server joined to Active Directory also have NetBios Name, defaulted to the 15 first characters of the computerName attribute.
                        Administrators can specify a custom NetBios Name for a SMB server using this attribute.
                        NetBios Name are limited to 15 characters and can not contain the following characters:
                        - backslash (\)
                        - slash mark (/)
                        - colon (:)
                        - asterisk (*)
                        - question mark (?)
                        - quotation mark ("")
                        - less than sign ()
                        - greater than sign (>)
                        - vertical bar (|)
                        as definied by the Microsoft naming convention (see https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/909264/)

workgroup.title: workgroup
workgroup.description:
Applies to stand-alone SMB servers Only.
                        Windows network workgroup for the SMB server.
                        Worlgroup Names are limited to 15 alphanumeric ASCII characters.

host_iosize.title: host_iosize
host_iosize.description:
Typical size of writes from the server or other computer using the file system or LUN to the storage system. This setting is used to match the storage block size to the I/O of the primary application using the storage, which can optimize performance. Choose one of the suggested applications if you are using one of them, or check the setup guide or performance tuning information for the primary application, or stay with default 8K size for general use.  For create operation applies only to vmware datastore fstype filesystems:
                        - 8192: General_8K - Host I/O size is 8K for general purpose
                        - 16384: General_16K - Host I/O size is 16K for general purpose
                        - 32768: General_32K - Host I/O size is 32K for general purpose
                        - 65536: General_64K - Host I/O size is 64K for general purpose

nas_server.title: nas_server
nas_server.description:
Id of associated Nas Server instance that uses this LDAP object.
                        Only one LDAP object per Nas Server is supported.

authentication_type.title: authentication_type
authentication_type.description:
Authentication type for the LDAP server.
                        - 0 - Anonymous => Anonymous authentication means no authentication occurs and the Nas Server uses an anonymous login to access the LDAP-based directory server.
                        - 1 - Simple => Simple authentication means the Nas Server must provide a bind distinguished name and password to access the LDAP-based directory server.
                        - 2 - Kerberos => Kerberos authentication means the Nas Server uses a KDC to confirm the identity when accessing the Active Directory.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Anonymous**
* **Simple**
* **Kerberos**

base_dn.title: base_dn
base_dn.description:
Base Distinguished Name. This is the root of the LDAP directory tree where the SDNAS server will query information.
                        The base DN must be expressed in X.509 format by using the attribute dc=. For example, if the fully-qualified domain name is mycompany.com, the base DN is dc=mycompany,dc=com.

addresses.title: addresses
addresses.description: IP addresses of the LDAP servers.

items.title: addresses
items.description: IP addresses of the LDAP servers.

port_number.title: port_number
port_number.description:
The TCP/IP port used by the Nas Server to connect to the LDAP servers.
                        Default value for LDAP is 389 and LDAPS is 636.

protocol.title: protocol
protocol.description:
Indicates whether the LDAP protocol uses SSL for secure network communication. SSL encrypts data over the network and provides message and server authentication.
                        - 0 - ldap => LDAP protocol without SSL.
                        - 1 - ldaps => (Default) LDAP protocol with SSL. When you enable LDAPS, make sure to specify the appropriate LDAPS port (usually port 636) and to upload an LDAPS trust certificate to the LDAP server.
                        - 2 - unknown => Unknown protocol.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **ldap**
* **ldaps**
* **unknown**

verify_server_certificate.title: verify_server_certificate
verify_server_certificate.description:
Indicates whether Certification Authority certificate is used to verify the LDAP server certificate for secure SSL connections. Values are:
                        - {true} - verifies LDAP server's certificate.
                        - {false} - doesn't verify LDAP server's certificate.

profile_dn.title: profile_dn
profile_dn.description: For an iPlanet LDAP server, specifies the DN of the entry with the configuration profile.

bind_dn.title: bind_dn
bind_dn.description: Bind Distinguished Name (DN) to be used when binding.

smb_account_used.title: smb_account_used
smb_account_used.description:
Indicates whether SMB authentication is used to authenticate to the LDAP server. Values are:
                        - {true} - Indicates that the SMB settings are used for Kerberos authentication.
                        - {false} - Indicates that Kerberos uses its own settings.

principal.title: principal
principal.description: Specifies the principal name for Kerberos authentication.

realm.description: Specifies the realm name for Kerberos authentication.
