items.title: metrics
items.description:
metrics

 Enumeration values:
* **AvgWPDiscTime** - Avg WP Disconnect Time (ms).
* **AvgRDFSWriteResponseTime** - Avg RDF Write Time (ms).
* **AvgReadMissResponseTime** - Avg RDF Write Time (ms).
* **PercentBusy** - The percent of time the directory is busy.
* **HostIOs** - A host command for data transfer.
* **HostMBs** - The size of the data transfer from the host in MBs per second.
* **Reqs** - The data transfer between the director and the cache. An IO may require multiple requests depending on IO size, alignment or both. The requests rate should be either equal to or greater than the IO rate.
* **ReadReqs** - A read data transfer between the director and the cache. An IO may require multiple requests depending on IO size, alignment or both. The requests rate should be either equal to or greater than the IO rate.
* **WriteReqs** - A write data transfer between the director and the cache. An IO may require multiple requests depending on IO size, alignment or both. The requests rate should be either equal to or greater than the IO rate.
* **HitReqs** - A request that is immediately serviced from the cache (instead of having to wait for the data to arrive, or destage from the disk.)
* **ReadHitReqs** - A read request that is immediately serviced from the cache (instead of having to wait for the data to arrive from the disk.)
* **WriteHitReqs** - A write request that is immediately serviced from the cache (instead of having to wait for the data to be destaged to the disk.)
* **MissReqs** - A request that is a miss.
* **ReadMissReqs** - A read request that cannot be satisfied immediately from the cache and needs to wait for the data to arrive from the disk.
* **WriteMissReqs** - A write request that cannot be satisfied immediately from the cache and needs to wait for the data to be destaged to the disk.
* **PercentReadReqs** - The percent of read requests out of host commands for data transfer.
* **PercentWriteReqs** - The percent of write requests out of host commands for data transfer.
* **PercentHitReqs** - The percent of requests that are served from cache.
* **PercentReadReqHit** - The percent of read requests that are served from cache.
* **SystemWPEvents** - A write miss due to the system write pending limit having been reached.
* **DeviceWPEvents** - A write miss due to the volume write pending limit having been reached.
* **SyscallCount** - The total number of calls seen by this director.
* **AvgTimePerSyscall** - The average time that it took to serve one system call.
* **SyscallRemoteDirCounts** - The number of calls per second sent from the local director to another director in the same system.
* **Syscall_RDF_DirCounts** - The number of calls per second sent via RDF to a remote system.
* **SlotCollisions** - A write miss due to the desired cache slot being locked by another request.
* **PercentWriteReqHit** - The percent of write requests that were satisfied from cache.
* **TotalReadCount** - The total number of reads to the FE directors.
* **TotalWriteCount** - The total number of writes to the FE directors.
* **ReadResponseTime** - A calculated average response time for reads.
* **WriteResponseTime** - A calculated average response time for writes.
* **HostIOLimitIOs** - The number of IOs per second that are executed by the director under IO quota limits.
* **HostIOLimitMBs** - The throughput in MBs that are transferred by the director under IO quota limits.
* **OptimizedReadMisses** - Number of read requests each second performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **OptimizedMBReadMisses** - Number of host MBs read each second directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **AvgOptimizedReadMissSize** - The average read size of the read requests performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **QueueDepthUtilization** - Front end director queue depth utilization, based on queue depth buckets
* **AvgQueueDepthRange0** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. Based on depth, the applicable queue depth bucket is incremented with the value seen by the IO. For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.
* **AvgQueueDepthRange1** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. Based on depth, the applicable queue depth bucket is incremented with the value seen by the IO. For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.

For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.

For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.

For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.
* **AvgQueueDepthRange5** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. Based on depth, the applicable queue depth bucket is incremented with the value seen by the IO. For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.
* **AvgQueueDepthRange6** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. Based on depth, the applicable queue depth bucket is incremented with the value seen by the IO. For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.
* **AvgQueueDepthRange7** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. Based on depth, the applicable queue depth bucket is incremented with the value seen by the IO. For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.
* **AvgQueueDepthRange8** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. Based on depth, the applicable queue depth bucket is incremented with the value seen by the IO. For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.
* **AvgQueueDepthRange9** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. Based on depth, the applicable queue depth bucket is incremented with the value seen by the IO. For example, an IO that encounters a queue depth of 7 will increment bucket #2 (depth 5-9 for OS or 7-14 for MF) by 7.
* **QueueDepthCountRange0** - As an IO enters the queue it first checks how deep the queue is. After incrementing the queue depth bucket, it increments the associated count bucket by 1. This then represents the number of IOs that encountered queues of certain depth during the past time interval. The intent of these buckets is to identify IO bursts which in turn generate large queues and long response times.

metrics.title: metrics
metrics.description:
metrics

 Enumeration values:
* **TotalTracksSpilledBack** - Total Tracks Spilled Back.
* **TotalTracksSpilledOver** - Total Tracks Spilled Over.
* **TotalTracksSpilledOverUsed** - Total Tracks Spilled over used.
* **SpillOverPackedSlots** - Spillover Packed Slots.
* **AvgCycleTime** - The mean time (in secs) of the last 16 cycles. (Calculated as true averages- e.g. if only 8 cycles have occurred, the average represents those 8 cycles only.)
* **LastCycleDuration** - The cycle time (in secs) of the most recently completed cycle. It should be noted that in a regular case the cycles switch every ~30 sec, however, in most cases the collection interval is in minutes, which means some cycle times will be skipped. This an important counter to look at to figure out if SRDF/A is working as expected.
* **TotalHAWrites** - The t+C16otal host adapter writes, measured in write commands to SRDF/A volumes only.
* **TotalHARepeatWrites** - The number of writes to a slot already in the active cycle. Total host adapter repeat writes, measured in write commands to SRDF/A volumes only. This counter helps estimate the cache locality of reference, i.e., how much cache is saved by the re-writes. This does not give any indication to the bandwidth locality of reference.
* **RDF_R1_R2_IOs** - The number of IOs/sec being transmitted across the links for this session.
* **RDF_R1_R2_MBs** - The number of MB/sec being transmitted across the links for this session.
* **RDF_R2_R1_IOS** - The number of IOs/sec being transmitted across the links for this session.
* **RDF_R2_R1_MBs** - The number of MB/sec being transmitted across the links for this session.
* **HostWrites** - The number of write IOs/sec coming in from the hosts for the volumes in this session.
* **HostMBWritten** - The throughput in MB/sec of writes coming in from the hosts for the volumes in this session.
* **SystemWPLimit** - The maximum number of write-pending slots for the entire Symmetrix system.
* **SystemWPCount** - The number of cache slots marked as write pending for the system.
* **LocalWPCount** - The number of write pending slots waiting to be de-staged to disk. On the R1 - should be less than or equal to the system write pending count.
* **RDFA_WPCount** - The number of RDF/A writes pending.
* **Percent_RDFA_MaxCacheUsage** - The parameter establishing the percent of system write pendings at which an RDF/A session will drop.
* **MinCycleTime** - The setting for the minimum number of seconds for a cycle.
* **DSEUsedTracks** - The active cycle spilled count plus the inactive cycle spilled count.
* **Percent_R1_UsedCache** - The ratio of system write pending count to the maximum write pending slots. This is the percent of cache used by this RDF/A group.
* **Percent_R2_UsedCache** - The ratio of system write pending count to the maximum write pending slots. This is the percent of cache used by this RDF/A group.
* **TotalReads** - The number of tracks read from the R2 (host reads + copy).
* **MBReads** - The amount of data read from the R2 in MBs+G5.
* **TimeSinceLastSwitch** - The time (in secs) since the last switch from active to inactive or vice versa. Since in a regular case the cycles switch every ~30 sec and the samples are taken every few minutes, this counter may not show very significant data, however, when cycles elongate beyond the sample time, this counter can help indicate an estimate of the R1 to R1 lag time.
* **ActiveCycleSize** - The count of cache slots that measures the amount of data captured (on the R1 side) or received (on the R2 side).
* **InactiveCycleSize** - The count of cache slots that measures the amount of data transmitted (on the R1 side) or applied (on the R2 side).
* **RDFA_SessionIndicator** - Indicates if group is RDFA (0/1).
* **RDFA_ActiveIndicator** - Indicates if RDF/A group is active (0/1).
* **SessionPriority** - The order in which SRDF/A groups are dropped out of SRDF/A in the event of a cache limit being reached. The highest priority is 1, the lowest is 64, and the default is 33.
* **CycleNumber** - The active cycle number identifier for the given SRDF/A session. In a regular case, the cycles switch every ~30 seconds, however, in most cases the collection interval is in minutes. Calculating the difference in cycle numbers between collection intervals relative to the minimum cycle time will show how many cycles occurred over the last interval.
* **HighWaterMark** - The greatest number of cache slots transmitted (on the R1 side) or applied (on the R2 side) in any cycle so far in the session.
* **R1_R2_Lag_Time** - The time that R2 is behind R1 (RPO). This is calculated as the last cycle time plus the time since last switch. In a regular case, the cycles switch every ~30 sec and the samples are taken every few minutes, therefore this counter may not show very significant data, however, when cycles elongate beyond the sample time, this counter can help indicate an estimate of the RPO.
* **NumDevsInSession** - The number of Symmetrix volumes defined in this session.
* **R2_LastRestoreTime** - The  time, in seconds, that the restore of the last active cycle took to complete. The restore is an operation done by the DA and RA to mark the tracks in the apply cycle as Write Pending to the local drives. This operation is usually a very quick cache operation, unless one of the volumes reaches its WP limit. The importance of this counter is that is shows one of the common reasons for an increase in the cycle time.
* **R2_AvgRestoreTime** - The mean time (in secs) of the last 16 restores. (Calculated as true averages, e.g., if only 8 restores have occurred, the average represents those 8 restores only.)
* **ConsistencyProtection** - Specifies whether consistency protection is set.
* **DSEThreshold** - DSE Threshold
* **SessionNumber** - The number of the SRDF/A session.
* **UncommittedTracks** - The number of tracks for all the SRDF/A volumes in the session that have not been committed to the R2 volume.
* **TotalWrites** - The number of write requests to the RDF director per second.
* **MBWritten** - The number of MBs written per second for the SRDF/A session.
* **ReadHits** - The total number of read operations that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **MaxCycleTime** - The setting for the maximum number of seconds for a cycle.
* **AvgIOServiceTime** - The average time it took to satisfy reads and writes.
* **AvgCycleSize** - The average size of all the cycles in RDFA sessions.
* **NumberOfLinks** - The number of links used by this RDF/A group.
* **NumberOfCompressedLinks** - The number of compressed links used by this RDF/A group.
* **CompressedMBReceived** - The number of compressed MBs/sec received by this RDF/A group.
* **CompressedMBSent** - The percent of compressed MBs/sec sent by this RDF/A group.
* **CompressedMBSentAndReceived** - The number of compressed MBs sent and received by this RDF/A group.
* **PercentCompressedMBReceived** - Calculated value: ((Compressed MBs Received/sec /MBs Received/sec) * 100)
* **PercentCompressedMBSent** - Calculated value: ((Compressed MBs Sent/sec / MBs Sent/sec) * 100)
* **PercentCompressedMBSentAndReceived** - Calculated value: ((Compressed MBs Sent and Received/sec / MBs Sent and Recieved/sec) * 100)
* **NumberCyclesBehind** - Number of cycles the R2 is behind the R1
* **CycleNumberTransmitApply** - Active cycle number identifier for the given SRDF/A session on the R2 side
* **XtremSWCacheIOs** - The number of IOs per second received in the XtremSW Cache.
* **PercentXtremSWCacheReadHit** - The percent of total XtremSW Cache read IO operations performed each second by all of the Symmetrix volumes that were served by the XtremSW Cache cache.
* **XtremSWCacheMBs** - Cumulative number of host MBs read and written by the StremSW Cache per second.
* **XtremSWCacheResponseTime** - The average time it took the XtremSW cache to serve one IO.
* **OptimizedReadMisses** - Number of read requests each second performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **OptimizedMBReadMisses** - Number of host MBs read each second directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **AvgOptimizedReadMissSize** - The average read size of the read requests performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **RdfReads** - RDF Reads.
* **RdfWrites** - RDF Writes.
* **RdfMBRead** - RDF MB Read.
* **RdfMBWritten** - RDFMB Written.
* **RdfReadHits** - RDF Read Hits.
* **RDFRewrites** - RDF Rewrites.
* **IOServiceTimeLong** - RDF Avg IO Service Time Long ms.
* **IOServiceTimeShort** - RDF Avg IO Service Time Short.
* **MaxIOServiceTime** - RDF Max IO Service Time ms.
* **MinIOServiceTime** - RDF Min IO Service Time ms.
* **RdfResponseTime** - RDF Response Time ms.
* **NumberOfRDFInvalidTracks** - Number Of RDF Invalid Tracks.
* **CapacityTracksSpilledBack** - CapacityTracksSpilledBack
* **CapacityTracksSpilledOverUsed** - Capacity Tracks Spilled Over Used
* **CapacityTracksSpilledOver** - Capacity Tracks Spilled Over
* **SpillOverPackedSlotsCapacity** - Spillover Packed Slots Capacity
* **UncommittedTracksCapacity** - Uncommitted Tracks Capacity
* **LocalWPUsedCapacity** - Local WP Used Capacity
* **RDFAWPCountCapacity** - RDF/A WP Count Capacity
* **ActiveCycleCapacitySize** - Active Cycle Capacity Size
* **InactiveCycleCapacitySize** - Inactive Cycle Capacity Size
* **CapacityOfRDFInvalidTracks** - Capacity of RDF Invalid Tracks
* **DSEThresholdCapacity** - DSE Threshold Capacity
* **DSEUsedTracksCapacity** - DSE Threshold Capacity
* **AvgCycleSizeCapacity** - Avg Cycle Size Capacity

metrics.title: metrics
metrics.description:
metrics

 Enumeration values:
* **PercentBusy** - The percent of time the directory is busy.
* **IOs** - The average IO count per second for the director.
* **Reqs** - The total read and write requests per second.
* **ReadReqs** - The read requests per second.
* **WriteReqs** - The write requests per second.
* **MBWritten** - The size of the host data transfer in MBs for the director.
* **MBRead** - The size of the data received in MBs for the director.
* **AvgIOSizeReceived** - Calculated value: (MBs received per sec / reads per sec)
* **AvgIOSizeSent** - Calculated value: (MBs sent per sec / writes per sec)
* **SyscallCount** - The total number of calls seen by this director.
* **SyscallTime** - The time to satisfy the calls by this director.
* **SyscallRemoteDirCount** - The number of calls sent from the local director to another director in the same system.
* **Syscall_RDF_DirCounts** - The number of calls sent via RDF to a remote system.
* **AvgTimePerSyscall** - Calculated value: (syscall time / syscall count)
* **Rewrites** - Rewrites/sec
* **MBSentAndReceived** - The total read and write MBs on the RDF director per sec.
* **WriteMissReqs** - The write misses for the RDF director per second. A miss occurs when the request has to wait for data to destage from cache to disk.
* **AvgIOServiceTime** - The average time the director takes to serve IO.
* **CopyIOs** - The number of copy IOs per second via this RDF director.
* **CopyMBs** - The number of copy MBs per second via this RDF director.
* **SyncWrites** - The number of sync write requests managed by this RDF director per second.
* **SyncMBSent** - The number of sync MBs sent per second via this RDF director.
* **AsyncWriteReqs** - The number of async write requests managed by this RDF director per second.
* **AsyncMBSent** - The number of async MBs sent per second via this RDF director.
* **TracksSentPerSec** - Tracks Sent Per Sec.
* **TracksReceivedPerSec** - Tracks Sent Per Sec.
* **CompresedMBSentAndReceived** - Compressed MBs Sent and Received/sec
* **CompresedMBReceived** - Compressed MBs Received/sec
* **CompresedMBSent** - Compressed MBs Sent/sec
* **NumberOfCompresedLinks** - Number of Compressed Links
* **NumberOfLinks** - Number of Links
* **PercentCompresedMBSentAndReceived** - % Compressed MBs Sent and Received
* **PercentCompresedMBReceived** - % Compressed MBs Received
* **PercentCompresedMBSent** - % Compressed MBs Sent

metrics.title: metrics
metrics.description:
metrics

 Enumeration values:
* **SpillOverPackedSlots** - Spillover Packed Slots.
* **HostIOs** - The number of host operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostReads** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostWrites** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostHits** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadHits** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteHits** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostMisses** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that could not be satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadMisses** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteMisses** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostMBs** - Cumulative number of host MBs read/writes per second by the group.
* **HostMBReads** - The cumulative number of host MBs read per second by the group.
* **HostMBWritten** - The cumulative number of host MBs written per second by the group.
* **BEReqs** - The number of read/write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEReadReqs** - The number of read requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEWriteReqs** - The number of write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **ReadResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one read IO for this group.
* **WriteResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write IO for this group.
* **ReadMissResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one read miss IO for this group.
* **WriteMissResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write miss IO for this group.
* **RDFS_WriteResponseTime** - A summary of the read, write, and average response times for the selected SRDF/S group.
* **PercentRead** - The percent of IO operations that were reads.
* **PercentWrite** - The percent of IO operations that were writes.
* **PercentReadHit** - The percent of read operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentWriteHit** - The percent of write operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentReadMiss** - The percent of read miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the requested read data is not found in cache.
* **PercentWriteMiss** - The percent of write miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the write operation had to wait while data was destaged from cache to the disks.
* **WPCount** - The number of tracks currently in write pending mode for the group
* **SeqIOs** - The number of IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqReads** - The number of read IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqWrites** - The number of write IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqReadHits** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **SeqReadMisses** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **SeqWriteHits** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **SeqWriteMisses** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **RandomIOs** - The number of IOs from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReads** - The number of read IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomWrites** - The number of write IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReadHits** - The number of random read IOs that were satisfied from the cache.
* **RandomWriteHits** - The number of random write IOs that were immediately placed in cache because space was available.
* **RandomReadMisses** - The number of random read IOs that were misses.
* **RandomWriteMisses** - The number of random write IOs that were misses.
* **AvgIOSize** - Calculated value: (HA Kbytes transferred per sec/total IOs per sec)
* **AvgReadSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes read per sec/total reads per sec)
* **AvgWriteSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes written per sec/total writes per sec)
* **MaxWPThreshold** - The maximum number of write-pending slots available for the group.
* **BEMBTransferred** - The number of MBs read per second + MBs written per second.
* **BEMBReads** - The number of MBs read by the disk directors from the disk each second.
* **BEMBWritten** - The number of MBs written to the disk from the disk director each second.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackss** - The total prefetched tracks each second from the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackUsed** - The number of prefetched tracks used each second from the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEReadRequestTime** - The average time it takes to make a request by the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEDiskReadResponseTime** - The average time it takes cache to respond to a read request by the disk directors.
* **BEReadTaskTime** - The time from the point when the HA puts the read request on the queue and the DA picks it up - can be considered queue time.
* **PercentHit** - The percent of IO operations that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **PercentMisses** - The percent of IO operations that were misses.
* **ResponseTime** - The average response time for the reads and writes.
* **UsedCapacity** - The capacity in GBs of this group.
* **BlockSize** - The block size in KBs of the devices in this group.
* **BEPrefetchedMBs** - The number of MBs prefetched from disk to cache in a second.
* **IODensity** - The number of back-end requests per GB of disk.
* **BERDFCopyThroughput** - BE RDF Copy (MB)/ BE RDF Copy Throughput
* **BEThroughputReadForCopy** - BE Reads For Copy (KB)/ BE Throughput Read For Copy
* **BEWriteThroughputForCopy** - BE Writes For Copy (KB)/ BE Write Throughput For Copy
* **BEWriteThroughputForRebuild** - BE Writes For Rebuild (KB)/ BE Write Throughput For Rebuild
* **BEOptimizeWriteThroughput** - BE Optimize Writes (KB)/ BE Optimize Write Throughput
* **BEPartialSectorThroughputWritten** - BE Partial Sector Writes (KB)/ BE Partial Sector Throughput Written
* **BERDFCopy** - BE RDF Copy
* **BEReadsForCopy** - BE Reads For Copy
* **BEWritesForCopy** - BE Writes For Copy
* **BEWritesForRebuild** - BE Writes For Rebuild
* **BEXORThroughputRead** - BE XOR Read (KB)/ BE XOR Throughput Read
* **BEXORReads** - BE XOR Reads
* **RDFResponseTime** - RDF Response Time (ms)/ RDF Response Time
* **WPUsedCapacity** - WP Used Capacity
* **MaxWPCapacityThreshold** - Capacity of the maximum number of write-pending slots available
* **XtremSWCacheIOs** - The number of IOs per second received in the XtremSW Cache.
* **PercentXtremSWCacheReadHit** - The percent of total XtremSW Cache read IO operations performed each second by all of the Symmetrix volumes that were served by the XtremSW Cache cache.
* **XtremSWCacheMBs** - Cumulative number of host MBs read and written by the StremSW Cache per second.
* **XtremSWCacheResponseTime** - The average time it took the XtremSW cache to serve one IO.
* **OptimizedReadMisses** - Number of read requests each second performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **OptimizedMBReadMisss** - Number of host MBs read each second directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **AvgOptimizedReadMissSize** - The average read size of the read requests performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **WritePacedDelay** - The total delay in milliseconds that the RDF directors impose on RDF devices in order to better serve remote IOs.
* **AvgWritePacedDelay** - The average time in milliseconds that host writes on RDF devices are delayed by the RDF directors.
* **TotalCapacityGB** - Total Capacity (GB).
* **RdfWritesPerSec** - RDF Writes/sec.
* **RdfReadsPerSec** - RDF Reads/sec.
* **RdfRewritesPerSec** - RDF Rewrites/sec.
* **RdfReadHitsPerSec** - RDF Read Hits/sec.
* **RdfMBsWrittenPerSec** - RDF MBs Written/sec.
* **RdfMBsReadPerSec** - RDF MBs Read/sec.
* **BEPercentReads** - BE % Reads
* **BEPercentWrites** - BE % Writes
* **PercentRandomIO** - % Random IO
* **PercentRandomReads** - % Random Reads
* **PercentRandomReadHit** - % Random Read Hit
* **PercentRandomReadMiss** - % Random Read Miss
* **PercentRandomWrites** - Random Writes
* **PercentRandomWriteHit** - % Random Write Hit
* **PercentRandomWriteMiss** - % Random Write Miss
* **PercentSeqIO** - % Sequential IO
* **PercentSeqRead** - % Seq Read
* **PercentSeqReadHit** - % Seq Read Hit
* **PercentSeqReadMiss** - % Seq Read Miss
* **PercentSeqWrites** - % Seq Writes
* **PercentSeqWriteHit** - % Seq Write Hit
* **PercentSeqWriteMiss** - % Seq Write Miss

category.title: category
category.description: The Category Endpoint required for Arrays,Directors, Ports and Storage Groups

instanceId.title: instanceId
instanceId.description:
If Category is of type director the instanceId will be required in the following format e.g. FA-5D .
                                If Category is of type port the instanceId will be required in the following format e.g. FA-7D:28 .
                                If Category is of type array , no instanceId is required .

keys.title: keys
keys.description: Instance name can be an Array Id(e.g.00019780XXXX), Director ID(e.g. FA-1D), or a Port ID(e.g. FA-1D:4)

 Enumeration values:
* **PercentReads** - Percentage of file system internal read I/O relative to all file systems.

* **WriteThroughput** - Bytes of file system internal write I/O
* **IOPS** - node.*.storage.filesystem.*.writes
                        + node.*.storage.filesystem.*.reads
* **Throughput** - node.*.storage.filesystem.*.readBytes
                        + node.*.storage.filesystem.*.writeBytes
* **ReadResponseTime** - node.*.storage.filesystem.*.clientReadTimeAvg
* **WriteResponseTime** - node.*.storage.filesystem.*.clientWriteTimeAvg
* **ResponseTime** - ReadTime = "node.*.storage.filesystem.*.clientReadTimeAvg" * "node.*.storage.filesystem.*.reads"
                        WriteTime = "node.*.storage.filesystem.*.clientWriteTimeAvg" * "node.*.storage.filesystem.*.writes"
                        (Read Time + Write Time)/(Reads + Writes)
* **AverageIOSize** - (node.*.storage.filesystem.*.readBytes + node.*.storage.filesystem.*.writeByte)/(node.*.storage.filesystem.*.read+node.*.storage.filesystem.*.write)

* **WriteResponseTimeCount2** - Write Response time Count 1 to 3.

* **WriteResponseTimeCount4** - Write Response time Count 5 to 8.

* **WriteResponseTimeCount6** - Write Response time Count 14 to 32
* **WriteResponseTimeCount7** - Write Response time Count Over 32.
* **Skew** - Skew.
* **HostIOs** - The number of host operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostReads** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostWrites** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostHits** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadHits** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteHits** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostMisses** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that could not be satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadMisses** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteMisses** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostMBs** - Cumulative number of host MBs read/writes per second by the group.
* **HostMBReads** - The cumulative number of host MBs read per second by the group.
* **HostMBWritten** - The cumulative number of host MBs written per second by the group.
* **BEReqs** - The number of read/write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEReadReqs** - The number of read requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEWriteReqs** - The number of write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **ReadResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one read IO for this group.
* **WriteResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write IO for this group.
* **ReadMissResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one read miss IO for this group.
* **WriteMissResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write miss IO for this group.
* **RDFS_WriteResponseTime** - A summary of the read, write, and average response times for the selected SRDF/S group.
* **PercentRead** - The percent of IO operations that were reads.
* **PercentWrite** - The percent of IO operations that were writes.
* **PercentReadHit** - The percent of read operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentWriteHit** - The percent of write operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentReadMiss** - The percent of read miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the requested read data is not found in cache.
* **PercentWriteMiss** - The percent of write miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the write operation had to wait while data was destaged from cache to the disks.
* **WPCount** - The number of tracks currently in write pending mode for the group
* **SeqIOs** - The number of IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqReads** - The number of read IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqWrites** - The number of write IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqReadHits** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **SeqReadMisses** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **SeqWriteHits** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **SeqWriteMisses** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **RandomIOs** - The number of IOs from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReads** - The number of read IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomWrites** - The number of write IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReadHits** - The number of random read IOs that were satisfied from the cache.
* **RandomWriteHits** - The number of random write IOs that were immediately placed in cache because space was available.
* **RandomReadMisses** - The number of random read IOs that were misses.
* **RandomWriteMisses** - The number of random write IOs that were misses.
* **AvgIOSize** - Calculated value: (HA Kbytes transferred per sec/total IOs per sec)
* **AvgReadSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes read per sec/total reads per sec)
* **AvgWriteSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes written per sec/total writes per sec)
* **MaxWPThreshold** - The maximum number of write-pending slots available for the group.
* **BEMBTransferred** - The number of MBs read per second + MBs written per second.
* **BEMBReads** - The number of MBs read by the disk directors from the disk each second.
* **BEMBWritten** - The number of MBs written to the disk from the disk director each second.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackss** - The total prefetched tracks each second from the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackUsed** - The number of prefetched tracks used each second from the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEReadRequestTime** - The average time it takes to make a request by the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEDiskReadResponseTime** - The average time it takes cache to respond to a read request by the disk directors.
* **BEReadTaskTime** - The time from the point when the HA puts the read request on the queue and the DA picks it up - can be considered queue time.
* **PercentHit** - The percent of IO operations that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **PercentMisses** - The percent of IO operations that were misses.
* **ResponseTime** - The average response time for the reads and writes.
* **AllocatedCapacity** - The capacity in GBs of this group.
* **BlockSize** - The block size in KBs of the devices in this group.
* **BEPrefetchedMBs** - The number of MBs prefetched from disk to cache in a second.
* **IODensity** - The number of back-end requests per GB of disk.
* **ExternalReadResponseTime** - The time it takes to process read request outside the system
* **ExternalResponseTime** - The time it takes to process IO request outside the system
* **ExternalWriteResponseTime** - The time it takes to process write request outside the system
* **InternalReadResponseTime** - The time it takes to process Internal Read RT request inside the system
* **InternalResponseTime** - The time it takes to process IO request inside the system
* **InternalWriteResponseTime** - The time it takes to process Internal Write RT request inside the system
* **XtremSWCacheIOs** - The number of IOs per second received in the XtremSW Cache.
* **PercentXtremSWCacheReadHit** - The percent of total XtremSW Cache read IO operations performed each second by all of the Symmetrix volumes that were served by the XtremSW Cache cache.
* **XtremSWCacheMBs** - Cumulative number of host MBs read and written by the StremSW Cache per second.
* **XtremSWCacheResponseTime** - The average time it took the XtremSW cache to serve one IO.
* **OptimizedReadMisses** - Number of read requests each second performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **OptimizedMBReadMisss** - Number of host MBs read each second directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **AvgOptimizedReadMissSize** - The average read size of the read requests performed directly from disks bypassing the cache.
* **WritePacedDelay** - The total delay in milliseconds that the RDF directors impose on RDF devices in order to better serve remote IOs.
* **AvgWritePacedDelay** - The average time in milliseconds that host writes on RDF devices are delayed by the RDF directors.
* **TotalTracks** - The total number of allocated tracks.
* **CompressedTracks** - The total number of tracks that are compressed.

                        Note: This metric invalid for 5977 and 5978, and should be
                        treated as legacy stats (5876 and below).
* **PercentCompressedTracks** - The percent of tracks that are compressed.

                        Note: This metric invalid for 5977 and 5978, and should be
                        treated as legacy stats (5876 and below).
* **InfoAlertCount** - Number of active performance alerts, information severity
* **WarningAlertCount** - Number of active performance alerts, warning severity
* **CriticalAlertCount** - Number of active performance alerts, critical severity
* **SRDFS_WriteReqs** - Sync Write Reqs/sec
* **SRDFS_MBSent** - Sync MBs Sent/sec
* **SRDFA_WriteReqs** - Async Write Reqs/sec
* **SRDFA_MBSent** - Async MBs Sent/sec
* **HostIOLimitPercentTimeExceeded** - Host IO Limit % Time Exceeded.
* **RdfReads** - RDF Reads.
* **RdfWrites** - RDF Writes.
* **RdfMBRead** - RDF MB Read.
* **RdfMBWritten** - RDFMB Written.
* **RdfReadHits** - RDF Read Hits.
* **RDFRewrites** - RDF Rewrites.

* **HostIOs** - The number of host operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostReads** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostWrites** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostHits** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadHits** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteHits** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostMisses** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that could not be satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadMisses** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteMisses** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostMBs** - Cumulative number of host MBs read/writes per second by the group.
* **HostMBReads** - The cumulative number of host MBs read per second by the group.
* **HostMBWritten** - The cumulative number of host MBs written per second by the group.
* **BEReqs** - The number of read/write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEReadReqs** - The number of read requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEWriteReqs** - The number of write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.

* **WriteResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write IO for this group.

* **WriteMissResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write miss IO for this group.

* **PercentRead** - The percent of IO operations that were reads.
* **PercentWrite** - The percent of IO operations that were writes.
* **PercentReadHit** - The percent of read operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentWriteHit** - The percent of write operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentReadMiss** - The percent of read miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the requested read data is not found in cache.
* **PercentWriteMiss** - The percent of write miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the write operation had to wait while data was destaged from cache to the disks.
* **WPCount** - The number of tracks currently in write pending mode for the group
* **SeqIOs** - The number of IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqReads** - The number of read IO operations performed each second that were sequential.

* **SeqReadMisses** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **SeqWriteHits** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **SeqWriteMisses** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **RandomIOs** - The number of IOs from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReads** - The number of read IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomWrites** - The number of write IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReadHits** - The number of random read IOs that were satisfied from the cache.
* **RandomWriteHits** - The number of random write IOs that were immediately placed in cache because space was available.
* **RandomReadMisses** - The number of random read IOs that were misses.
* **RandomWriteMisses** - The number of random write IOs that were misses.
* **AvgIOSize** - Calculated value: (HA Kbytes transferred per sec/total IOs per sec)
* **AvgReadSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes read per sec/total reads per sec)
* **AvgWriteSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes written per sec/total writes per sec)

* **BEMBWritten** - The number of MBs written to the disk from the disk director each second.

* **InternalResponseTime** - The time it takes to process IO request inside the system
* **InternalWriteResponseTime** - The time it takes to process Internal Write RT request inside the system

* **RandomWriteHits** - The number of random write IOs that were immediately placed in cache because space was available.

* **RandomWriteMisses** - The number of random write IOs that were misses.

* **AvgWriteSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes written per sec/total writes per sec)

* **BEMBWritten** - The number of MBs written to the disk from the disk director each second.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackss** - The total prefetched tracks each second from the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackUsed** - The number of prefetched tracks used each second from the disk directors to the cache.

* **BEDiskReadResponseTime** - The average time it takes cache to respond to a read request by the disk directors.

* **PercentMisses** - The percent of IO operations that were misses.

metrics

 Enumeration values:

* **AvgWriteResponseTime7** - Write Response Time Over 32.

* **ReadResponseTimeCount6** - Read Response time Count 14 to 32.
* **ReadResponseTimeCount7** - Read Response time Count Over 32.
* **WriteResponseTimeCount1** - Write Response time Count 0 to 1.
* **WriteResponseTimeCount2** - Write Response time Count 1 to 3.
* **WriteResponseTimeCount3** - Write Response time Count 3 to 5.
* **WriteResponseTimeCount4** - Write Response time Count 5 to 8.
* **WriteResponseTimeCount5** - "Write Response time Count 8 to 14.
* **WriteResponseTimeCount6** - Write Response time Count 14 to 32
* **WriteResponseTimeCount7** - Write Response time Count Over 32.
* **Skew** - Skew.
* **HostIOs** - The number of host operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostReads** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostWrites** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group.
* **HostHits** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadHits** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteHits** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **HostMisses** - The number of host read/write operations performed each second by the group that could not be satisfied from cache.
* **HostReadMisses** - The number of host read operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostWriteMisses** - The number of host write operations performed each second by the group that were not satisfied from cache.
* **HostMBs** - Cumulative number of host MBs read/writes per second by the group.
* **HostMBReads** - The cumulative number of host MBs read per second by the group.
* **HostMBWritten** - The cumulative number of host MBs written per second by the group.
* **BEReqs** - The number of read/write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEReadReqs** - The number of read requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **BEWriteReqs** - The number of write requests each second performed by the disk directors to cache.
* **ReadResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one read IO for this group.
* **WriteResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write IO for this group.
* **ReadMissResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one read miss IO for this group.
* **WriteMissResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one write miss IO for this group.
* **RDFS_WriteResponseTime** - A summary of the read, write, and average response times for the selected SRDF/S group.
* **PercentRead** - The percent of IO operations that were reads.
* **PercentWrite** - The percent of IO operations that were writes.
* **PercentReadHit** - The percent of read operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentWriteHit** - The percent of write operations, performed by the group, that were immediately satisfied by cache.
* **PercentReadMiss** - The percent of read miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the requested read data is not found in cache.
* **PercentWriteMiss** - The percent of write miss operations performed each second by the group. A miss occurs when the write operation had to wait while data was destaged from cache to the disks.
* **WPCount** - The number of tracks currently in write pending mode for the group
* **SeqIOs** - The number of IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqReads** - The number of read IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqWrites** - The number of write IO operations performed each second that were sequential.
* **SeqReadHits** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **SeqReadMisses** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **SeqWriteHits** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **SeqWriteMisses** - The number of sequential write operations performed each second by the group that were misses.
* **RandomIOs** - The number of IOs from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReads** - The number of read IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomWrites** - The number of write IO commands from a host not identified as part of a sequential stream.
* **RandomReadHits** - The number of random read IOs that were satisfied from the cache.
* **RandomWriteHits** - The number of random write IOs that were immediately placed in cache because space was available.
* **RandomReadMisses** - The number of random read IOs that were misses.
* **RandomWriteMisses** - The number of random write IOs that were misses.
* **AvgIOSize** - Calculated value: (HA Kbytes transferred per sec/total IOs per sec)
* **AvgReadSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes read per sec/total reads per sec)
* **AvgWriteSize** - Calculated value: (Kbytes written per sec/total writes per sec)
* **MaxWPThreshold** - The maximum number of write-pending slots available for the group.

* **BEMBReads** - The number of MBs read by the disk directors from the disk each second.
* **BEMBWritten** - The number of MBs written to the disk from the disk director each second.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackss** - The total prefetched tracks each second from the disk directors to the cache.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackUsed** - The number of prefetched tracks used each second from the disk directors to the cache.

* **BEDiskReadResponseTime** - The average time it takes cache to respond to a read request by the disk directors.
* **BEReadTaskTime** - The time from the point when the HA puts the read request on the queue and the DA picks it up - can be considered queue time.

* **PercentMisses** - The percent of IO operations that were misses.

* **BlockSize** - The block size in KBs of the devices in this group.

* **WritePacedDelay** - The total delay in milliseconds that the RDF directors impose on RDF devices in order to better serve remote IOs.

* **BEReadResponseTime** - The average time it took the disk to serve one read command (DATA pool).
* **BEWriteResponseTime** - The average time it took the disk to serve one write command (DATA pool).
* **BEAvgResponseTime** - The average time it took the disk to service IO (DATA pool).
* **IngressTracks** - The number of tracks entering the pool.
* **EgressTracks** - The number of tracks leaving the pool.
* **TotalTracks** - The total number of tracks of the virtual pool.
* **CompressedTracks** - The total number of tracks that are compressed.

                        Note: This metric invalid for 5977 and 5978, and should be
                        treated as legacy stats (5876 and below).
* **PercentCompressedTracks** - The number of compressed tracks of the virtual pool.

                        Note: This metric invalid for 5977 and 5978, and should be
                        treated as legacy stats (5876 and below).
* **WPUsedCapacity** - WP Used Capacity
* **BEPercentReads** - BE % Reads.
* **BEPercentWrites** - BE % Writes.
* **IODensity** - IO Density.
* **BEPrefetchedTracks** - BE Prefetched Tracks.
* **BEPrefetchedTrackUsed** - BE Prefetched Tracks Used/sec
* **CompressionScanReads** - Compression Scan Reads.

metrics.title: metrics
metrics.description:
metrics

 Enumeration values:
* **HostMBs** - The size of the data transfer from the host in MBs per second.
* **MBRead** - The reads per second in MBs.
* **MBWritten** - The writes per second in MBs.
* **IoRate** - The number of host read and write requests for the disk.
* **Reads** - The number of host reads per second for the disk.
* **Writes** - The number of host writes per second for the disk.
* **ResponseTime** - Response Time (ms)
* **ReadResponseTime** - The average time that it took the Symmetrix to serve one read IO for this group.
* **WriteResponseTime** - The number of sequential read operations performed each second by the group that were immediately satisfied from cache.
* **WPCount** - The number of system cache slots that are write pending.
* **WPUsedCapacity** - Write Pending Used Capacity

                        for ucode
                        >= 5773.

average_cycle_time.description:
Average cycle time (seconds)
                        When the R1 side is reachable, it is the average time that a cycle spends as as the Capture (active) cycle. This includes the
                        overhead
                        of deciding to do the switch.
                        If the R1 is not reachable, it is the average time that a cycle spends as the Apply (active) cycle on the R2 side.

                        valid if

sym_force.title: sym_force
sym_force.description:
Requests the System force operation be executed when normally it is rejected. Use extreme caution when using
                                this
                                option.

                                CAUTION: Use care when applying symforce, as data could be lost or corrupted. Use of this option is not recommended,
                                except in
                                an emergency.

                                NOTE: To enable symforce, a parameter called SYMAPI_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE in the options file must be set to TRUE and
                                restart the
                                Unisphere server to pick up the change.

                                When used with symforce, a split command executes on an SRDF pair, even when the pair is sync in progress or restore in
                                progress.

                                specified
                                operation.

symForce.title: symForce
symForce.description:
Requests the System force operation be executed when normally it is rejected. Use extreme caution when using
                                this
                                option.

                                CAUTION: Use care when applying symforce, as data could be lost or corrupted. Use of this option is not recommended,
                                except in
                                an emergency.

                                NOTE: To enable symforce, a parameter called SYMAPI_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE in the options file must be set to TRUE and
                                restart the
                                Unisphere server to pick up the change.

                                When used with symforce, a split command executes on an SRDF pair, even when the pair is sync in progress or restore in
                                progress.
                                During the execution of an establish or restore command, -symforce prohibits the verification of valid tracks on the
                                device at
                                the source.

star.title: star
star.description: Targets the action at volumes in STAR mode.

bypass.title: bypass
bypass.description:
Bypasses any existing System exclusive locks during an SRDF operation.
                                WARNING: Only use this flag if you are certain no other SRDF operation is in progress at the local and/or remote
                                System.

exempt.title: exempt
exempt.description:
Allows volumes to be added, removed, or suspended without affecting the state of the SRDF/A or SRDF/Metro session or
                                requiring
                                that other volumes in the session be suspended. Used for an SRDF group supporting an active SRDF/A session or an active
                                SRDF/Metro session.

keepR1.title: keepR1
keepR1.description:
Sets the winner side of the SRDF/Metro group to the R1 or the R2 side, as specified. This option can be used when moving
                                volumes
                                out of the SRDF/Metro group but not when moving volumes into the group.

keepR2.title: keepR2
keepR2.description:
Sets the winner side of the SRDF/Metro group to the R1 or the R2 side, as specified. This option can be used when moving
                                volumes
                                out of the SRDF/Metro group but not when moving volumes into the group.

force.title: force

Attempts to force the operation even though one or more volumes may not be
                                in the normal, expected state(s) for the
                                specified
                                operation.

symforce.title: symforce
symforce.description:
Requests the System force operation be executed when normally it is
                                rejected. Use extreme caution when using
                                this
                                option.

                                CAUTION: Use care when applying symforce, as data could be lost or corrupted. Use of
                                this option is not recommended,
                                except in
                                an emergency.

                                NOTE: To enable symforce, a parameter called SYMAPI_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE in the options
                                file must be set to TRUE and
                                restart the
                                Unisphere server to pick up the change.

                                When used with symforce, a split command executes on an SRDF pair, even when the pair is
                                sync in progress or restore in
                                progress.
                                During the execution of an establish or restore command, -symforce prohibits the
                                verification of valid tracks on the
                                device at
                                the source.

* **IfRecommended** -

                        requested configuration change operation. If a performance capacity threshold is breached,
                        Return tested input object (with generated fields, if applicable), performance impact scores
                        for requested operation, and relevant warnings/errors.

                                is copied to the concurrent SRDF mirror. These
                                operations require the remote data copy option, or the concurrent link to be suspended.

immediate.title: immediate
immediate.description:
Applies only to SRDF/A-capable devices. Causes command to drop the SRDF/A
                                session immediately.

force.title: force
force.description:
Attempts to force the operation even though one or more volumes may not be
                                in the normal, expected state(s) for the
                                specified
                                operation.

symForce.title: symForce
symForce.description:
Requests the System force operation be executed when normally it is
                                rejected. Use extreme caution when using
                                this
                                option.

                                CAUTION: Use care when applying symforce, as data could be lost or corrupted. Use of
                                this option is not recommended,
                                except in
                                an emergency.

                                NOTE: To enable symforce, a parameter called SYMAPI_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE in the options
                                file must be set to TRUE and
                                restart the
                                Unisphere server to pick up the change.

                                When used with symforce, a split command executes on an SRDF pair, even when the pair is
                                sync in progress or restore in
                                progress.
                                During the execution of an establish or restore command, -symforce prohibits the
                                verification of valid tracks on the
                                device at
                                the source.

bypass.description:

                                WARNING: Only use this flag if you are certain no other SRDF operation is in progress at
                                the local and/or remote
                                System.

mode.title: mode
mode.description:
Sets the SRDF mode for one or more SRDF pairs to synchronous,
                                asynchronous, adaptive copy disk mode or adaptive copy
                                write pending mode.
                                Note: The skew cannot be used with the async value. The consistent option is only
                                allowed with the sync value.
                            

 Enumeration values:
* **AdaptiveCopyDisk**
* **AdaptiveCopyWP**
* **Synchronous**
* **Asynchronous**
* **Active**

skew.title: skew
skew.description:
Sets the skew factor for the adaptive copy mode. Possible values range
                                from 0 to 65,534 tracks. For devices larger than
                                2 GB, a value of 65,535 can be specified to target all the tracks of any given drive.

consistent.title: consistent
consistent.description: Transitions from async to sync mode.

symforce.title: symforce
symforce.description:
Forces the operation to execute when normally it is rejected. Use extreme caution with this option. If used when a link
                                is copy
                                in
                                progress or when a restore is restore in progress, this will cause an incomplete copy and data on the copy target would
                                not be
                                usable.

action.title: action
action.description:
The action to be performed.

 Enumeration values:
* **Restore** - Restore a snapshot generation
* **SetTimeToLive** - Set the number of days or hours for a snapshot generation before it auto-terminates
                        (provided it is not linked).
* **SetSecure** - Set the number of days or hours for a snapshot generation to be secure before it
                        auto-terminates (provided it is not linked).
* **Link** - Link a snapshot generation
* **Relink** - Relink a snapshot generation
* **Unlink** - Unlink a snapshot generation
* **SetMode** - Set the mode of a linked snapshot generation
* **Rename** - Rename a snapshot
* **Persist** - Persist a snapshot policy snapshot

symforce.title: symforce
symforce.description:
Forces the operation to execute when normally it is rejected. Use extreme caution with this option. If used when a link
                                is copy
                                in
                                progress or when a restore is restore in progress, this will cause an incomplete copy and data on the copy target would
                                not be
                                usable.

action.title: action
action.description:
The action to be performed.

 Enumeration values:
* **Restore** - Restore a snapshot generation
* **SetTimeToLive** - Set the number of days or hours for a snapshot generation before it auto-terminates
                        (provided it is not linked).
* **SetSecure** - Set the number of days or hours for a snapshot generation to be secure before it
                        auto-terminates (provided it is not linked).
* **Link** - Link a snapshot generation
* **Relink** - Relink a snapshot generation
* **Unlink** - Unlink a snapshot generation
* **SetMode** - Set the mode of a linked snapshot generation
* **Rename** - Rename a snapshot
* **Persist** - Persist a snapshot policy snapshot

performance_impact_validation_option.title: performance_impact_validation_option
performance_impact_validation_option.description:
performance_Impact_Validation_Option

 Enumeration values:
* **Preview** - 
                        Run performance impact tests and return performance impact scores for requested operation.
                        Return input object (with generated fields, if applicable) for relevant followup API.
                    
* **IfRecommended** - 
                        Run performance impact tests. If no performance capacity threshold is breached, run the
                        requested configuration change operation. If a performance capacity threshold is breached,
                        Return tested input object (with generated fields, if applicable), performance impact scores
                        for requested operation, and relevant warnings/errors.

bypass.description:
Bypasses any existing System exclusive locks during an SRDF operation.

                                System.

force.title: force

Attempts to force the operation even though one or more volumes may not be in the normal, expected state(s) for the
                                specified
                                operation.

symForce.title: symForce
symForce.description:
Requests the System force operation be executed when normally it is rejected. Use extreme caution when using
                                this
                                option.

                                CAUTION: Use care when applying symforce, as data could be lost or corrupted. Use of this option is not recommended,
                                except in
                                an emergency.

                                NOTE: To enable symforce, a parameter called SYMAPI_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE in the options file must be set to TRUE and
                                restart the
                                Unisphere server to pick up the change.

                                When used with symforce, a split command executes on an SRDF pair, even when the pair is sync in progress or restore in
                                progress.
                                During the execution of an establish or restore command, -symforce prohibits the verification of valid tracks on the
                                device at
                                the source.

star.description: Targets the action at volumes in STAR mode.

                                WARNING: Only use this flag if you are certain no other SRDF operation is in progress at the local and/or remote
                                System.

type.description:

* **DSE**
* **WritePacing**
* **WritePacingExempt**

                                swap
                                personality and an incremental restore is initiated.

skew.title: skew
skew.description:
Sets the skew factor for the adaptive copy mode. Possible values range from 0 to 65,534 tracks. For devices larger than
                                2 GB, a value of 65,535 can be specified to target all the tracks of any given drive.

consistent.title: consistent
consistent.description: Transitions from async to sync mode.

metroBias.title: metroBias
metroBias.description: Indicates that SRDF/Metro configuration should use Bias instead of Witness protection

keepR2.title: keepR2
keepR2.description: Sets the winner side of the SRDF/Metro group to the R1 or the R2 side, as specified.

new_rdfg_number.title: new_rdfg_number
new_rdfg_number.description: Specifies a new SRDF group in which to move a dynamic SRDF pair.

half_move.title: half_move
half_move.description: Moves one side of the dynamic SRDF pair from one SRDF group to another SRDF group.

force.title: force
force.description:
Attempts to force the operation even though one or more volumes may not be in the normal, expected state(s) for the
                                specified
                                operation.

symforce.title: symforce
symforce.description:
Requests the System force operation be executed when normally it is rejected.
                                Use extreme caution when using this option.

star.title: star
star.description: Targets the action at volumes in STAR mode.

hop2.title: hop2
hop2.description:
Targets the SRDF action at the group's second-hop volumes in a cascaded SRDF relationship. For example, in an RDF1
                                group, the
                                action
                                targets the R21->R2 pair of the R1->R21->R2 relationship.

bypass.title: bypass
bypass.description:
Bypasses any existing System exclusive locks during an SRDF operation.
                                WARNING: Only use this flag if you are certain no other SRDF operation is in progress at the local and/or remote
                                System.

exempt.title: exempt
exempt.description:
Allows devices to be added, removed, or suspended without affecting the state of the SRDF/A or SRDF/Metro session or
                                requiring
                                that other devices in the session be suspended. Used for an SRDF group supporting an active SRDF/A session or an active
                                SRDF/Metro session.

keepR1.title: keepR1
keepR1.description:
Sets the winner side of the SRDF/Metro group to the R1 or the R2 side, as specified. This option can be used when moving
                                volumes
                                out of the SRDF/Metro group but not when moving volumes into the group.

keepR2.title: keepR2
keepR2.description:
Sets the winner side of the SRDF/Metro group to the R1 or the R2 side, as specified. This option can be used when moving
                                volumes
                                out of the SRDF/Metro group but not when moving volumes into the group.

force.title: force
force.description:
Attempts to force the operation even though one or more volumes may not be in the normal, expected state(s) for the
                                specified
                                operation.

symForce.title: symForce
symForce.description:
Requests the System force operation be executed when normally it is rejected. Use extreme caution when using
                                this
                                option.

                                CAUTION: Use care when applying symforce, as data could be lost or corrupted. Use of this option is not recommended,
                                except in
                                an emergency.

                                NOTE: To enable symforce, a parameter called SYMAPI_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE in the options file must be set to TRUE and
                                restart the
                                Unisphere server to pick up the change.

                                When used with symforce, a split command executes on an SRDF pair, even when the pair is sync in progress or restore in
                                progress.
                                During the execution of an establish or restore command, -symforce prohibits the verification of valid tracks on the
                                device at
                                the source.

bypass.title: bypass
bypass.description:
Bypasses any existing System exclusive locks during an SRDF operation.
                                WARNING: Only use this flag if you are certain no other SRDF operation is in progress at the local and/or remote
                                System.

star.title: star
star.description: Targets the action at volumes in STAR mode.

hop2.title: hop2
hop2.description:

                                group, the
                                action
                                targets the R21->R2 pair of the R1->R21->R2 relationship.
