symapi_APPREG_EXPIRATION_PERIOD.title: symapi_APPREG_EXPIRATION_PERIOD
symapi_APPREG_EXPIRATION_PERIOD.description:
Application Registration Expiration Period.

                                                        Sets the number of days after which an Application Registration entry
                                                        will be expired. The allowable values for this option are from 15 to 365.
                                                        The default setting is 90 days.

symapi_ALLOW_CG_ENABLE_FROM_R2.title: symapi_ALLOW_CG_ENABLE_FROM_R2
symapi_ALLOW_CG_ENABLE_FROM_R2.description:
Allow Enabling Of CG From R2 Side.

                                                        Allows the enabling of CGs from the R2 side. The allowable values for this
                                                        option are ENABLE and DISABLE. The default setting is ENABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

symapi_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE.title: symapi_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE
symapi_ALLOW_RDF_SYMFORCE.description:
Allow Use of RDF -symforce Switch.

                                                        Indicates whether users can specify -symforce when performing RDF
                                                        control operations. The allowable values for this option are true and
                                                        false. The default setting is false.

symapi_CG_TIMEOUT.title: symapi_CG_TIMEOUT
symapi_CG_TIMEOUT.description:
CG Timeout.

                                                        Sets the time-out period during which the host will attempt to trip a
                                                        failed consistency group. The allowable values for this option are from 10
                                                        to 120. The default setting is 30 seconds.

symapi_CLONE_COPY_ON_WRITE.title: symapi_CLONE_COPY_ON_WRITE
symapi_CLONE_COPY_ON_WRITE.description:
Clone Copy on Write.

                                                        Sets clone nocopy mode to copy-on-write (ENABLE) or copy-on-access
                                                        (DISABLE). The allowable values for this option are ENABLE and DISABLE.
                                                        The default setting is DISABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

symapi_COMMAND_SCOPE.title: symapi_COMMAND_SCOPE
symapi_COMMAND_SCOPE.description:
Command Scope.

                                                        Sets clone nocopy mode to copy-on-write (ENABLE) or copy-on-access
                                                        (DISABLE). The allowable values for this option are ENABLE and DISABLE.
                                                        The default setting is DISABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

symapi_DEFAULT_RDF_MODE.title: symapi_DEFAULT_RDF_MODE
symapi_DEFAULT_RDF_MODE.description:
Default RDF Mode:

                                                        Specifies the default rdf_mode for createpair operations. The allowable
                                                        values for this option are SYNC or ACP. The default setting is ACP.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Sync**
* **Acp**

symapi_GNS_MIRRORED_GROUP_CONTROL.title: symapi_GNS_MIRRORED_GROUP_CONTROL
symapi_GNS_MIRRORED_GROUP_CONTROL.description:
GNS Mirrored Group Control:

                                                        Allows group modifications to device groups that were created by a remote
                                                        GNS daemon via remote SRDF mirroring.  The allowable values for this option
                                                        are ENABLE and DISABLE. The default setting is DISABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

symapi_POOL_DRAIN_THRESHOLD.title: symapi_POOL_DRAIN_THRESHOLD
symapi_POOL_DRAIN_THRESHOLD.description:
Pool Drain Threshold:

                                                        This option can be set to change the Pool used space threshold that is
                                                        validated when disabling or draining a save or data device.  If the used
                                                        space of a pool will exceed this threshold after the drain or disable
                                                        request is satisfied, then the request will be blocked. The allowable
                                                        values for this option are from 0 to 100 percent. The default setting is
                                                        90.

symapi_RCOPY_GET_MODIFIED_TRACKS.title: symapi_RCOPY_GET_MODIFIED_TRACKS
symapi_RCOPY_GET_MODIFIED_TRACKS.description:
Rcopy Get Modified Tracks:

                                                        Enables/disables the calculation of modified tracks. The allowable
                                                        values for this option are true or false. The default setting is
                                                        false.

symapi_RDF_CHECK_R2_NOT_WRITABLE.title: symapi_RDF_CHECK_R2_NOT_WRITABLE
symapi_RDF_CHECK_R2_NOT_WRITABLE.description:
RDF Check R2 Not Writable:

                                                        When enabled, verify that the R2 devices are not writable by the host for
                                                        createpair operations other than createpair -invalidate [R1 | R2]. The
                                                        allowable values for this option are ENABLE or DISABLE. The default setting
                                                        is DISABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

symapi_RDF_CREATEPAIR_LARGER_R2.title: symapi_RDF_CREATEPAIR_LARGER_R2
symapi_RDF_CREATEPAIR_LARGER_R2.description:
RDF Createpair Larger R2:

                                                        The allowable values for this option are ENABLE or DISABLE. The default
                                                        setting is ENABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

symapi_RDF_RW_DISABLE_R2.title: symapi_RDF_RW_DISABLE_R2
symapi_RDF_RW_DISABLE_R2.description:
RDF Read/Write Disable R2:

                                                        Enabling this option causes the R2 device to be set to read/write disabled,
                                                        or not-ready, on the RA during establish, restore, failback, or
                                                        createpair-establish operations. The allowable values for this option are
                                                        ENABLE or DISABLE. The default setting is DISABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

symapi_SNAP_COUNT_MODIFIED_TRACKS.title: symapi_SNAP_COUNT_MODIFIED_TRACKS
symapi_SNAP_COUNT_MODIFIED_TRACKS.description:
SNAP Count Modified Tracks:

                                                        Sets snap to return a count of the changed tracks. This option is ignored
                                                        when the request is made remotely. The allowable values for this option are
                                                        true or false. The default setting is false.

symapi_TF_COUNT_MODIFIED_TRACKS.title: symapi_TF_COUNT_MODIFIED_TRACKS
symapi_TF_COUNT_MODIFIED_TRACKS.description:
TimeFinder Count Modified Tracks:

                                                        Sets TimeFinder to return a count of the changed tracks. This option is
                                                        ignored when the request is made remotely. The allowable values for this
                                                        option are true or false. The default setting is false.

symapi_USE_ACCESS_ID.title: symapi_USE_ACCESS_ID
symapi_USE_ACCESS_ID.description:
Use Access ID:

                                                        This option applies to Symmetrix Access Control. It specifies whether to
                                                        use the access ID generated on client or server. Used only on the server
                                                        side during client/server operations. Possible values are:

                                                        CLIENT: The client access ID is used for every command performed. If a
                                                        client access ID is not available the command will fail.
                                                        SERVER: The server access ID is used for every command performed.
                                                        ANY:    If the client access ID is available it is used for every command
                                                        performed. If it is not available then the server access ID is
                                                        used.

                                                        The default setting is SERVER.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Any**
* **Client**
* **Server**

log_Level.title: log_Level
log_Level.description:
Log Level

                                                        Specifies a severity-based control over logging volume. Messages that are
                                                        issued with a severity equal to or exceeding the level specified will be
                                                        recorded in the log file. Do not use debug or verbose without direction
                                                        from EMC Customer Support. Value maybe error, info, debug, verbose, warning.

                                                        The default is warning.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Error**
* **Warning**
* **Info**
* **Verbose**
* **Debug**

logfile_Retention.title: logfile_Retention
logfile_Retention.description:
LogFIle Retention:

                                                        Log files will be deleted when the number days indicated in this option
                                                        have passed since the log file was last updated. The value of this option
                                                        is in days. The default is 3 days.

log_Event_Syslog_Host.title: log_Event_Syslog_Host
log_Event_Syslog_Host.description:
Log Event Syslog Server:

                                                        Specifies the host on which the remote syslog server is running. This
                                                        option is for event logging to syslog (Log Event Targets option should
                                                        contain 'syslog'). This can be either a host name or an IP address.
                                                        There is no default.

log_Event_Syslog_Port.title: log_Event_Syslog_Port
log_Event_Syslog_Port.description:
Log Event Syslog Port:

                                                        Specifies the port to which the remote syslog server is listening.
                                                        This option is for event logging to syslog (Log Event Targets option
                                                        should contain 'syslog'). The value for this options should be a decimal
                                                        port number. The default value is 514.

gns_Device_Poll_Interval.title: gns_Device_Poll_Interval
gns_Device_Poll_Interval.description:
GNS Device Poll Interval

                                                        Specifies how frequently storgnsd polls for changes made to groups from other
                                                        hosts. The smaller this value is, the more quickly a host will recognize group
                                                        changes made from other hosts. The values is in seconds. The default is 15
                                                        seconds.

gns_Ppath_Poll_Interval.title: gns_Ppath_Poll_Interval
gns_Ppath_Poll_Interval.description:
GNS PPATH Poll Interval

                                                        Specifies how frequently storgnsd checks to see whether PowerPath or RDF
                                                        consistency group updates are necessary. The value specified is in seconds.
                                                        The default is 60 seconds.

gns_Remote_Mirror.title: gns_Remote_Mirror
gns_Remote_Mirror.description:
GNS Remote Mirror

                                                        Specifies whether storgnsd should automatically mirror RDF1 and RDF2 type
                                                        DG and CG group definitions for use on remote (SRDF) Symmetrix arrays.
                                                        Limitations include:
                                                        - REGULAR type and type ANY groups are not mirrored.
                                                        - Mirrors will not be created if the remote
                                                        Symmetrix array is also directly attached to the local host. (Refer to
                                                        the GNS_SYMAVOID_LOCAL option below.)
                                                        - For CG groups, BCV type devices are not mirrored.
                                                        The values for this option is enable and disable. The default value is disable.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

gns_Rmtarr_Update_Interval.title: gns_Rmtarr_Update_Interval
gns_Rmtarr_Update_Interval.description:
GNS Remote Array Update Interval

                                                        Specifies how frequently storgnsd should attempt to propagate device groups
                                                        over to remote (SRDF) Symmetrix arrays. This is only relevant if the GNS
                                                        Remote Mirror option is set to enable. The value for this option is in
                                                        seconds. The default is 60 seconds.

log_Level.title: log_Level
log_Level.description:
Log Level

                                                        Specifies a severity-based control over logging volume. Messages that are
                                                        issued with a severity equal to or exceeding the level specified will be
                                                        recorded in the log file. Do not use debug or verbose without direction
                                                        from EMC Customer Support. Value maybe error, info, debug, verbose, warning.
                                                        The default is warning.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Error**
* **Warning**
* **Info**
* **Verbose**
* **Debug**

logfile_Retention.description:
LogFIle Retention

                                                        Log files will be deleted when the number days indicated in this option
                                                        have passed since the log file was last updated. The value of this option
                                                        is in days. The default is 3 days.

log_Level.title: log_Level
log_Level.description:
Log Level:

                                                        Specifies a severity-based control over logging volume. Messages that are
                                                        issued with a severity equal to or exceeding the level specified will be
                                                        recorded in the log file. Do not use debug or verbose without direction
                                                        from EMC Customer Support. Value maybe error, info, debug, verbose, warning.

                                                        The default is info.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Error**
* **Warning**
* **Info**
* **Verbose**
* **Debug**

logfile_Retention.title: logfile_Retention
logfile_Retention.description:
LogFIle Retention:

                                                        Log files will be deleted when the number days indicated in this option
                                                        have passed since the log file was last updated. The value of this option
                                                        is in days. The default is 3 days.

log_Filter.title: log_Filter
log_Filter.description:
Log Filter:

                                                        Specifies category-based control over logging volume, by selecting
                                                        which types of storsrvd numbered log messages are recorded in the
                                                        storsrvd log files. Each log message issued by storsrvd is associated
                                                        with a category, expressed by the filter names shown below.
                                                        The numbered messages will appear in the storsrvd log files.

                                                        Numbered messages issued by storsrvd use a message identifier
                                                        that precedes the message text:
                                                        ANR0001I SYMAPI server ready to accept SECURE connections.
                                                        The value to be specified for LOG_FILTER consists of a series of
                                                        keywords shown below separated by a commas (,) or plus signs {+).

                                                        SERVER: Records high level events of the server: initialization,
                                                        termination, and other events not directly related to a SYMAPI session.

                                                        SESSION: Records arrival and termination of SYMAPI sessions.

                                                        APIREQ: Records arrival and completion of SYMAPI function requests.

                                                        CONTROLS: Records activity during execution of management requests
                                                        from stordaemon or the system console in the z/OS environment.

                                                        If LOG_FILTER specifies either SESSION or APIREQ it is most useful
                                                        to set LOG_LEVEL to info.
                                                        The default for this option is SESSION+APIREQ.

security_Clt_Secure_Lvl.title: security_Clt_Secure_Lvl
security_Clt_Secure_Lvl.description:
Client certificate verification level:

                                                        This option controls the verification of the client certificate by the
                                                        server. The following values may be specified:


                                                        MUSTVERIFY: Indicates that the server will only accept communications
                                                        from a version of the client that can send a certificate to be verified.

                                                        NOVERIFY: Indicates that the server will not verify the client
                                                        certificate.

                                                        VERIFY: Indicates that the server will verify a client certificate if
                                                        the version of the client can send a certificate.

                                                        The default for this option is NOVERIFY.
                                                        THIS OPTION IS IGNORED IF SECURE COMMUNICATIONS ARE NOT ESTABLISHED.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Verify**
* **Mustverify**
* **Noverify**

max_Sessions.title: max_Sessions
max_Sessions.description:
Maximum SYMAPI sessions to the server:

                                                        This option will take precedence over the options file option
                                                        SYMAPI_MAX_CLIENTS. This specifies the global high-water mark of all
                                                        sessions allowed by the server, without respect to source host or user.
                                                        When the next new session will cause the current number of sessions to
                                                        exceed this number, it will be refused. Allowable values for this option
                                                        are between 0 and 100 inclusive. The default value for this option is 100.

max_Sessions_Per_Host.title: max_Sessions_Per_Host
max_Sessions_Per_Host.description:
Maximum SYMAPI sessions per host to the server:

                                                        This option specifies the high-water mark for concurrent sessions from any
                                                        specific host. When the next new session from the source host will cause
                                                        the current number of sessions from that host to exceed this number, the
                                                        session will be refused.

                                                        When setting a numeric value, it should be the MAX_SESSIONS value. If it is
                                                        set greater than MAX_SESSIONS, then the maximum number of sessions for any
                                                        specific host will be restricted to MAX_SESSIONS.

                                                        If the value is set to NOLIMIT, then host sessions are only restricted by
                                                        the MAX_SESSIONS value.

security_Cert_Allow_Wildcards.title: security_Cert_Allow_Wildcards
security_Cert_Allow_Wildcards.description:
Wildcards in Common Name:

                                                        Specifies if wildcards are allowed in the Common Name in client certificates.
                                                        The following values may be specified:

                                                        ENABLE: Indicates that the server will accept wildcards in the Common Name
                                                        of a client certificate.

                                                        DISABLE: Indicates that the server will reject any host with wildcards in
                                                        the Common Name of a client certificate.

                                                        The default value for this option is DISABLE.
                                                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Enable**
* **Disable**

items.title: real_time_storage_groups
items.description:

alert_warning.description: Issue Alert if Threshold is exceeded

first_threshold_occurrences.title: first_threshold_occurrences
first_threshold_occurrences.description:
The number of occurrences in the data samples which must happen before the
                                alert is triggered. For example, if the threshold is breached three times out of five
                                samples, an alert is initiated.

first_threshold_samples.title: first_threshold_samples
first_threshold_samples.description:
The number of data collection samples over which the occurrences can occur before the
                                alert is triggered. For example, if the threshold is breached three times out of five
                                samples, an alert is initiated. The samples are based on the STP (DCF file) collection
                                interval for performance stats. This interval is typically five minutes by default, but
                                you can choose to collect in 10 minutes or 15 minute intervals. The samples are based
                                on the performance collection interval, so typically one sample = one 5 min interval. As
                                Unisphere processes the statistics to save to the performance database, it checks to see
                                if any performance thresholds have been breached. For example, if after processing three
                                5 min intervals and the threshold for a particular statistic was breached in each of
                                those intervals, then an alert would be triggered.

first_threshold_severity.title: first_threshold_severity
first_threshold_severity.description:
The following values are available:
                                INFORMATION
                                WARNING
                                CRITICAL
                            

 Enumeration values:
* **INFORMATION**
* **WARNING**
* **CRITICAL**

                                alert is triggered. For example, if the threshold is breached three times out of five

second_threshold_samples.title: second_threshold_samples
second_threshold_samples.description:
The number of data collection samples over which the occurrences can occur before the
                                alert is triggered. For example, if the threshold is breached three times out of five
                                samples, an alert is initiated. The samples are based on the STP (DCF file) collection
                                interval for performance stats. This interval is typically five minutes by default, but
                                you can choose to collect in 10 minutes or 15 minute intervals. The samples are based
                                on the performance collection interval, so typically one sample = one 5 min interval. As
                                Unisphere processes the statistics to save to the performance database, it checks to see
                                if any performance thresholds have been breached. For example, if after processing three
                                5 min intervals and the threshold for a particular statistic was breached in each of
                                those intervals, then an alert would be triggered.

port_group_policy.title: port_group_policy
port_group_policy.description:
Create new or use existing port group.

 Enumeration values:
* **CreateNewPortGroup** - 
                        Create new port group for request. (Automatic port selection is only available
                        for Fibre (SCSI FC). If host initiators are detected to be iSCSI or NVME TCP, ports
                        will not be selected. A CreatePortGroupParam will be generated with a portGroupId,
                        but an empty symmetrixPortKey list. The symmetrixPortKey list will need to be manually
                        populated before attempting to create the masking view.)
                    
* **UseExistingPortGroup** - Select from existing port groups.

port_selection_policy.title: port_selection_policy
port_selection_policy.description:
Select ports or port group based on utilization only or consider existing zoning as well.

 Enumeration values:
* **UtilizationBased** - Select least utilized ports or port group.
* **ZoningBased** - Select least utilized ports or port group that has existing zoning in place for specified host initiators.

performance_impact_validation_option.title: performance_impact_validation_option
performance_impact_validation_option.description:
performance_Impact_Validation_Option

 Enumeration values:
* **Preview** - 
                        Run performance impact tests and return performance impact scores for requested operation.
                        Return input object (with generated fields, if applicable) for relevant followup API.
                    
* **IfRecommended** - 
                        Run performance impact tests. If no performance capacity threshold is breached, run the
                        requested configuration change operation. If a performance capacity threshold is breached,
                        Return tested input object (with generated fields, if applicable), performance impact scores
                        for requested operation, and relevant warnings/errors.

overall_efficiency_ratio_to_one.title: overall_efficiency_ratio_to_one
overall_efficiency_ratio_to_one.description:
Overall Efficiency Ratio is the ratio between the sum of subscribed and snapshot
                        capacity for user devices and the user used capacity.
                        Format: "1.0:1" will be returned as 1.0.

data_reduction_ratio_to_one.title: data_reduction_ratio_to_one
data_reduction_ratio_to_one.description:
Data Reduction Ratio is the ratio between the sum of owned and RDP logical (128K
                        track

                        compression enabled devices.

                                specified, FBA by default

                                match the

                                valid

                                volumes:

                                reset (not broadcast to all channels).

Enable/Disable the environmental error reporting by the storage system to the
                                host on the specific iSCSI Target.

disable_Q_Reset_On_Ua.title: disable_Q_Reset_On_Ua
disable_Q_Reset_On_Ua.description:
When enabled, a Unit Attention (UA) that is propagated from another director
                                does not flush the queue for this volume on this director.
                                Used for hosts that do not expect the queue to be flushed on a 0629 sense (only
                                on a hard reset).

soft_Reset.title: soft_Reset
soft_Reset.description: Supports SCSI soft reset on a iSCSI Target when enabled for a Bull/GCOS-7 host.

scsi_3.title: scsi_3
scsi_3.description:
Alters the inquiry data (when returned by any volume on the port) to report that
                                the PowerMax system supports the SCSI-3 protocol.
                                When disabled, the SCSI-2 protocol is supported.

scsi_Support1.title: scsi_Support1
scsi_Support1.description:
Provides a stricter compliance with SCSI standards for managing volume
                                identifiers, multi-port targets,
                                unit attention reports, and the absence of a volume at LUN 0.

spc2_Protocol_Version.title: spc2_Protocol_Version
spc2_Protocol_Version.description:
This flag should be enabled (default) in a Windows 2003 environment running
                                Microsoft HCT test version 12.1.
                                When setting this flag, the port must be offline.

isid_Protected.title: isid_Protected

                                to calculate the World Wide Name (WWN) in the Persistent Group Registration
                                (PGR) record.

Enumeration values: * **all** - All settings in the category * **alert_notification_settings** - Excludes alert notification settings - mail server configuration, email recipients, snmp target list * **performance_preference_settings** - Excludes performance preference settings from the export - custom performance preference settings * **performance_user_templates** - Excludes performance user runbooks from the export - user defined performance runbooks * **performance_metric_settings** - Excludes performance metrics from the export - custom KPI enablement

dataExclusionParam.title: storageGroupDataExclusionParam

exclusionType.title: exclusionType
exclusionType.description:
The data exclusion types that will be set. Valid values are OneTime,
                                Recurring, or Both.
                            

 Enumeration values:
* **OneTime** - Set a one-time (expiring) data exclusion. If this value is specified, and no
                        corresponding
                        epochTimestamp value is set, any previously set one-time exclusion will be removed. If a
                        recurring exclusion is also set, it will not be impacted.
                    
* **Recurring** - Set recurring (non-expiring) data exclusions. If this value is specified, and no
                        corresponding
                        excludedBucket value is set, any previously set recurring exclusion will be removed. If a
                        one-time exclusion is also set, it will not be impacted.
                    
* **Both** - Set both one-time (expiring) and recurring (non-expiring) data exclusion values.
                        If this value is
                        specified, and either corresponding excludedBucket or epochTimestamp is not set, that setting
                        will
                        be removed.

epochTimestamp.title: epochTimestamp
epochTimestamp.description:
Specifying an epoch timestamp results in a one-time (expiring) data
                                exclusion. Workload planner ignores all workload data before this timestamp.

excludedBucket.title: excludedBucket
excludedBucket.description:
Specifying bucket values results in recurring (non-expiring) data
                                    exclusions. A bucket is a four hour time window. A week has 42 buckets (0-indexed).
                                    Bucket 0 is Sunday 12am-4am, 1 is Sunday 4am-8am, etc.

items.title: excludedBucket
items.description:
Specifying bucket values results in recurring (non-expiring) data
                                    exclusions. A bucket is a four hour time window. A week has 42 buckets (0-indexed).
                                    Bucket 0 is Sunday 12am-4am, 1 is Sunday 4am-8am, etc.

dataExclusionParam.description:
Parameter to update workload planner data exclusions for a given storage group or a
                specific storage system.

lastProcessed.description: The most recent timestamp processed by workload planner.

nextUpdate.title: nextUpdate
nextUpdate.description:
The estimated number of seconds until service level compliance and headroom values
                        are calculated and updated. Processing is on a 30 minute timer.
                        If processing does not occur within 30 minutes, 60 seconds is returned as an indicator to "check
                        back later" until processing completes.

performance_data_processing_state.title: performance_data_processing_state
performance_data_processing_state.description:
Returns Current if system is registered for performance data collection and most
                        recently processed timestamp is no more than 4 hours old. Otherwise, returns Stale.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Current** - Data has been processed or updated within four hours of the expected
                        processing/update time.
                    
* **Stale** - Data has not been processed or updated within four hours of the expected
                        processing/update time.
                    
* **NoData** - Last processed timestamp was null.

compliance_calculation_state.title: compliance_calculation_state
compliance_calculation_state.description:
Returns Current if service level compliance states have been updated within 4
                        hours of the most recently processed performance data timestamp. Otherwise, returns Stale.
                    

 Enumeration values:
* **Current** - Data has been processed or updated within four hours of the expected
                        processing/update time.
                    
* **Stale** - Data has not been processed or updated within four hours of the expected
                        processing/update time.
                    
* **NoData** - Last processed timestamp was null.

effectiveHostIoLimitValues.title: effectiveHostIoLimitValues
effectiveHostIoLimitValues.description:
Effective Host I/O limits (IOPS or MBPS) in buckets for a given storage group if
                        limits are applied.
                        For standalone groups, the value will be static across all the 42 buckets.
                        For a child storage group with limits but no limits on the parent, the value will be static
                        across all the 42 buckets.
                        For a child storage group with no direct limits but limits set on the parent, the limit of any
                        given child would be the parent limit minus whatever the siblings are using.
                        For a child storage group with direct limits and limits on the parent, the limit of any given
                        child would be the more limiting of the parent limit minus whatever the siblings are using, or
                        the child storage groups own limit.

items.title: effectiveHostIoLimitValues
items.description:
Effective Host I/O limits (IOPS or MBPS) in buckets for a given storage group if
                        limits are applied.
                        For standalone groups, the value will be static across all the 42 buckets.
                        For a child storage group with limits but no limits on the parent, the value will be static
                        across all the 42 buckets.
                        For a child storage group with no direct limits but limits set on the parent, the limit of any
                        given child would be the parent limit minus whatever the siblings are using.
                        For a child storage group with direct limits and limits on the parent, the limit of any given
                        child would be the more limiting of the parent limit minus whatever the siblings are using, or
                        the child storage groups own limit.

complianceState.title: complianceState
complianceState.description:
The service level compliance state of the storage group.

 Enumeration values:
* **Stable** - Both the last four hour weighted response time and the last two week weighted
                        response time are under the
                        response time threshold for the storage group's assigned service level.
                    
* **Marginal** - Either the last four hour weighted response time or the last two week weighted
                        response time is under the
                        response time threshold for the storage group's assigned service level.
                    
* **Critical** - Neither the last four hour weighted response time nor the last two week weighted
                        response time are under the
                        response time threshold for the storage group's assigned service level.

                                of the same day of the week and the same 4 hour time window across the week.

responseTimeTarget.title: responseTimeTarget
responseTimeTarget.description:
By default, the average expected response time of the source storage group's
                        assigned Service Level
                        (and workload type, where applicable) will be saved in the Provisioning Template. Specifying a
                        response time target here overrides
                        the default setting. The response time target in ms to use when provisioning from this
                        provisioning template. The response time
                        target will be converted to the appropriate service level on the target system. If this value is
                        set to -1, the
                        system default service level will be used instead (Optimized on hybrid systems and Diamond on
                        all-Flash systems).

numberOfVolumes.title: numberOfVolumes
numberOfVolumes.description:
By default, the number of volumes in the source storage group will be used.
                        Specifying a number of volumes here overrides the default setting. If 0 is entered,
                        user will be required to specify a number of volumes at provisioning time.

eventType.title: eventType
eventType.description:
Type of compliance event.

 Enumeration values:
* **InitialComplianceState** - Initial state for the storage group.
* **ComplianceStateChange** - Compliance state for the storage group has changed.
* **OneTimeExclusion** - System or storage group one time exclusion has been set for the storage group.
                    
* **RecurringExclusion** - System level recurring exclusion buckets have been set for the storage group.
                    
* **ServiceLevelChange** - Service Level for the storage group changed.
* **SrpChange** - Storage Resource Pool for the storage group changed.
* **WorkloadTypeChange** - Workload type for the storage group changed.
* **MissingData** - Performance data for the storage group is not available.

create_masking_view_impact_param.title: create_masking_view_impact_param

masking_view_id.title: masking_view_id
masking_view_id.description:
Masking view name. If not specified, it will be generated from storage
                                group name.

enable_compliance_alerts.title: enable_compliance_alerts
enable_compliance_alerts.description:
Optional setting to enable Compliance Alerts on the Storage Group(s) in the
                                masking view that have the following characteristics:
                                Non Parent Storage Group,
                                Service level other than Optimized and None,
                                Contains non Gatekeepers volumes.

create_masking_view_impact_param.description: Input parameters for a create masking view performance impact test.

source_symmetrix_id.title: source_symmetrix_id
source_symmetrix_id.description: Source Array ID for storage group to be migrated.

auto_resolve_conflicts.title: auto_resolve_conflicts
auto_resolve_conflicts.description:
If not specified or set to false, any configuration-related conflicts encountered on a
                                candidate system
                                when running a performance impact test will prevent that system from being recommended.
                                If used with
                                details flag equals true, that system will be added to the list of unsuitable systems
                                (with the specific
                                conflict being the reason for unsuitability). If set to true, arrays with conflicts may
                                be recommended
                                (if performance impact score allow it).

                                If true for create masking view performance impact test, any masking container names
                                specified by the user
                                that encounter a name conflict on a given array will be used as the seed name for an
                                updated
                                resolved/generated name. If auto_resolve_conflicts is not specified, or set to false,
                                any user specified masking component name conflict will prevent that target array from
                                being recommended.

                                If true for migration performance impact test, and migration validation fails due to
                                missing zoning, a
                                zoning report and pre-create port group will be generated as part of the generated input
                                portion of the
                                result. Note that in this case, zoning MUST be performed before the generated input
                                payload will succeed
                                in the subsequent migration API call. If auto_resolve_conflicts is not specified, or set
                                to false, strict
                                zoning validation will be performed for each migration target candidate system. Any
                                validation error,
                                zoning or otherwise, will prevent that target array from being recommended.

performance_impact_param.description:
Imput parameter to calculate the performance impact of the specified configuration change
                across all the systems.

ConfigurationManagementParamType.title: generated_input_param
ConfigurationManagementParamType.description: The configuration management param with generated attributes (if applicable) for subsequent system-specific POST request

ZoningRequirementEntry.title: zoning_requirement
ZoningRequirementEntry.description:
Initiator ID to port list. Initiator ID does not have an active path to any front end port
                in the associated masking view's port group.  The port list identifies all ports in the port group that
                can be used to zone an active path.  Not all ports in the list need to be zoned to the initiator ID.
                However, one active path between a port and the initiator ID is required for migration.

ZoningRequirementPort.title: port
ZoningRequirementPort.description: Front end port key and wwn for zoning.

array_impact.title: array_impact
array_impact.description: Array impact scores across front end, back end and cache components and overall score for a given system.

component_impact.title: overall

current.title: current
current.description: Performance impact based on current performance statistics

reserved.title: reserved
reserved.description:
Performance impact based on current performance statistics plus reserved performance
                        capacity (for example, jobs in job list)

updated.title: updated
updated.description: Performance impact based on current performance statistics plus new request (plus reserved performance, if applicable)

error.title: error
error.description: Error or warning, if unable to calculate performance impact

component_impact.description: Performance impact details for a given component type.

recommended.title: recommended
recommended.description: Configuration request is recommended based on performance and configuration checks (if applicable)

rank.title: rank
rank.description: Ranking as a suitable target array for this request in a multi-system environment

performance_impact_score.title: performance_impact_score
performance_impact_score.description: Overall performance impact score for the request

zoning_guidance.title: zoning_guidance
zoning_guidance.description: High level zoning hint, if not all ports in port group and host initiators in host group have active paths

zoning_requirement.title: zoning_requirement
zoning_requirement.description:
Initiator ID to port list map. Each entry represents an initiator ID does
                                not have an active path to any front end port in the associated masking view's port group.
                                The port list identifies all ports in the port group that can be used to zone an active path.
                                Not all ports in the list need to be zoned to the initiator ID.
                                However, one active path to each initiator ID is required for migration.

generated_link.description: The generated link for subsequent system-specific POST request

performance_impact_result.description: Performance impact result details for a specific system.
